14	/-!
15	
16	# Closed immersions of schemes
17	
18	A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is a closed immersion if the underlying map of topological spaces
19	is a closed immersion and the induced morphisms of stalks are all surjective.
20	
21	## Main definitions
22	
23	* `IsClosedImmersion` : The property of scheme morphisms stating `f : X ⟶ Y` is a closed immersion.
24	
25	## TODO
26	
27	* Show closed immersions are precisely the proper monomorphisms
28	* Define closed immersions of locally ringed spaces, where we also assume that the kernel of `O_X →
29	  f_*O_Y` is locally generated by sections as an `O_X`-module, and relate it to this file. See
30	  https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01HJ.
31	
32	-/
33	
34	@[expose] public section
35	
36	universe v u
37	
38	open CategoryTheory Opposite TopologicalSpace Topology
39	
40	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
41	
42	/-- A morphism of schemes `X ⟶ Y` is a closed immersion if the underlying
43	topological map is a closed embedding and the induced stalk maps are surjective. -/
44	@[mk_iff]
45	class IsClosedImmersion {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop extends SurjectiveOnStalks f where
46	  isClosedEmbedding (f) : IsClosedEmbedding f
47	
48	alias Scheme.Hom.isClosedEmbedding := IsClosedImmersion.isClosedEmbedding
49	
50	@[deprecated (since := "2026-01-20")]
51	alias IsClosedImmersion.base_closed := Scheme.Hom.isClosedEmbedding
52	
53	namespace IsClosedImmersion
54	
55	lemma eq_inf : @IsClosedImmersion = (topologically IsClosedEmbedding) ⊓
56	    @SurjectiveOnStalks := by
57	  ext X Y f
58	  rw [isClosedImmersion_iff, and_comm]
59	  rfl
60	
61	lemma iff_isPreimmersion {X Y : Scheme} {f : X ⟶ Y} :
62	    IsClosedImmersion f ↔ IsPreimmersion f ∧ IsClosed (Set.range f) := by
63	  rw [isClosedImmersion_iff, isPreimmersion_iff, and_assoc, isClosedEmbedding_iff]
64	
65	lemma of_isPreimmersion {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsPreimmersion f]
66	    (hf : IsClosed (Set.range f)) : IsClosedImmersion f :=
67	  iff_isPreimmersion.mpr ⟨‹_›, hf⟩
68	
69	instance (priority := 900) {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsClosedImmersion f] : IsPreimmersion f :=
70	  (iff_isPreimmersion.mp ‹_›).1
71	
72	/-- Isomorphisms are closed immersions. -/
73	instance {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsIso f] : IsClosedImmersion f where
74	  isClosedEmbedding := Homeomorph.isClosedEmbedding <| TopCat.homeoOfIso (asIso f.base)
75	  stalkMap_surjective := fun _ ↦ (ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _).2
76	
77	instance (priority := low) {X Y : Scheme.{u}} [IsEmpty X] (f : X ⟶ Y) : IsClosedImmersion f :=
78	  .of_isPreimmersion _ (by rw [Set.range_eq_empty]; exact isClosed_empty)
79	
80	instance : MorphismProperty.IsMultiplicative @IsClosedImmersion where
81	  id_mem _ := inferInstance
82	  comp_mem f g _ _ := ⟨g.isClosedEmbedding.comp f.isClosedEmbedding⟩
83	
84	/-- Composition of closed immersions is a closed immersion. -/
85	instance comp {X Y Z : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsClosedImmersion f]
86	    [IsClosedImmersion g] : IsClosedImmersion (f ≫ g) :=
87	  MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderComposition.comp_mem f g inferInstance inferInstance
88	
89	/-- Composition with an isomorphism preserves closed immersions. -/
90	instance respectsIso : MorphismProperty.RespectsIso @IsClosedImmersion := by
91	  apply MorphismProperty.RespectsIso.mk <;> intro X Y Z e f hf <;> infer_instance
92	
93	instance {X : Scheme} (I : X.IdealSheafData) : IsClosedImmersion I.subschemeι :=
94	  .of_isPreimmersion _ (I.range_subschemeι ▸ I.support.isClosed)
95	
96	/-- Given two commutative rings `R S : CommRingCat` and a surjective morphism
97	`f : R ⟶ S`, the induced scheme morphism `specObj S ⟶ specObj R` is a
98	closed immersion. -/
99	theorem spec_of_surjective {R S : CommRingCat} (f : R ⟶ S) (h : Function.Surjective f) :
100	    IsClosedImmersion (Spec.map f) where
101	  isClosedEmbedding := PrimeSpectrum.isClosedEmbedding_comap_of_surjective _ _ h
102	  stalkMap_surjective x := by
103	    haveI : (RingHom.toMorphismProperty (fun f ↦ Function.Surjective f)).RespectsIso := by
104	      rw [← RingHom.toMorphismProperty_respectsIso_iff]
105	      exact RingHom.surjective_respectsIso
106	    apply (MorphismProperty.arrow_mk_iso_iff
107	      (RingHom.toMorphismProperty (fun f ↦ Function.Surjective f))
108	      (Scheme.arrowStalkMapSpecIso f x)).mpr
109	    exact RingHom.surjective_localRingHom_of_surjective f.hom h x.asIdeal
110	
111	/-- For any ideal `I` in a commutative ring `R`, the quotient map `specObj R ⟶ specObj (R ⧸ I)`
112	is a closed immersion. -/
113	instance spec_of_quotient_mk {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (I : Ideal R) :
114	    IsClosedImmersion (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (Ideal.Quotient.mk I))) :=
115	  spec_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
116	
117	/-- Any morphism between affine schemes that is surjective on global sections is a
118	closed immersion. -/
119	lemma of_surjective_of_isAffine {X Y : Scheme} [IsAffine X] [IsAffine Y] (f : X ⟶ Y)
120	    (h : Function.Surjective (f.appTop)) : IsClosedImmersion f := by
121	  rw [MorphismProperty.arrow_mk_iso_iff @IsClosedImmersion (arrowIsoSpecΓOfIsAffine f)]
122	  apply spec_of_surjective
123	  exact h
124	
125	/--
126	If `f ≫ g` and `g` are closed immersions, then `f` is a closed immersion.
127	Also see `IsClosedImmersion.of_comp` for the general version
128	where `g` is only required to be separated.

29	theorem RingHom.ker_isRadical_iff_reduced_of_surjective {S F} [CommSemiring R] [Semiring S]
30	    [FunLike F R S] [RingHomClass F R S] {f : F} (hf : Function.Surjective f) :
31	    (RingHom.ker f).IsRadical ↔ IsReduced S := by
32	  simp_rw [isReduced_iff, hf.forall, IsNilpotent, ← map_pow, ← RingHom.mem_ker]
33	  rfl
34	
35	theorem isRadical_iff_span_singleton [CommSemiring R] :
36	    IsRadical y ↔ (Ideal.span ({y} : Set R)).IsRadical := by
37	  simp_rw [IsRadical, ← Ideal.mem_span_singleton]
38	  exact forall_comm.trans (forall_congr' fun r => exists_imp.symm)
39	
40	theorem isNilpotent_iff_zero_mem_powers [Monoid R] [Zero R] {x : R} :
41	    IsNilpotent x ↔ 0 ∈ Submonoid.powers x := Iff.rfl
42	
43	section CommSemiring
44	
45	variable [CommSemiring R] {x y : R}
46	
47	/-- The nilradical of a commutative semiring is the ideal of nilpotent elements. -/
48	def nilradical (R : Type*) [CommSemiring R] : Ideal R :=
49	  (0 : Ideal R).radical
50	
51	theorem mem_nilradical : x ∈ nilradical R ↔ IsNilpotent x :=
52	  Iff.rfl
53	
54	theorem nilradical_eq_sInf (R : Type*) [CommSemiring R] :
55	    nilradical R = sInf { J : Ideal R | J.IsPrime } :=
56	  (Ideal.radical_eq_sInf ⊥).trans <| by simp_rw [and_iff_right bot_le]
57	
58	theorem nilpotent_iff_mem_prime : IsNilpotent x ↔ ∀ J : Ideal R, J.IsPrime → x ∈ J := by
59	  rw [← mem_nilradical, nilradical_eq_sInf, Submodule.mem_sInf]
60	  rfl

import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ChartIso
import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.Points

/-!
# Cross01 substrate — Lane B (𝔾ₘ-scaling) reusable lemmas

This file ships **substrate** lemmas needed to close the Lane B
`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` cocycle proof (path (III.c) of
`AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`) together with two off-target sorries
(`gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`) in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`.

The substrates are generic in the geometric situation and live in their own
file rather than inline so they can be re-used by the three GmScaling consumer
applications and, if upstreamed, by Mathlib itself.  Their construction
follows the recipe of `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` (§2) and the prover-facing
specification at `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`.

## Contents (this iter)

* `AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` —
  Substrate 1: the closed-immersion universal property phrased as a
  topological range containment (under reduced source / target hypotheses).

Substrate 2 (`gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain`, tensor-of-domains
over an algebraically-closed field via the polynomial / localisation iso
chain) is owed a later iter prover dispatch.
-/

set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.style.setOption false

universe u

open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Topology

noncomputable section

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace IsClosedImmersion

/--
**Closed immersion lift by range inclusion.**
For a closed immersion `i : Z ⟶ X` whose source `Z` is reduced and a
quasi-compact morphism `g : Y ⟶ X` whose source `Y` is reduced, `g` factors
through `i` if and only if the topological range of `g` is contained in the
topological range of `i`.

This is the topological-range form of the universal property of closed
immersions; the kernel-inequality form is `IsClosedImmersion.lift` /
`IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac`.  The two forms are interchanged by the Galois
connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support` on ideal sheaves
(`IdealSheafData.gc` / `IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support`).

This is **Substrate 1** of the Lane B Cross01 cocycle proof; see
`blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`,
`Theorem~\ref{thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset}`.
-/
theorem lift_iff_range_subset
    {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (i : Z ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion i] [IsReduced Z]
    (g : Y ⟶ X) [QuasiCompact g] [IsReduced Y] :
    (∃ h : Y ⟶ Z, h ≫ i = g) ↔ Set.range g.base ⊆ Set.range i.base := by
  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  · -- (⇒) Forward direction: range pushdown.
    rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩
    rintro _ ⟨y, rfl⟩
    refine ⟨h.base y, ?_⟩
    simp [Scheme.Hom.comp_base]
  · -- (⇐) Backward direction: the substantive content.
    intro hrange
    -- Helper: the kernel of a ring hom into a reduced ring is a radical ideal.
    have ker_isRadical : ∀ {R S : Type u} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S]
        [_root_.IsReduced S] (f : R →+* S), (RingHom.ker f).IsRadical := by
      intro R S _ _ _ f x hx
      obtain ⟨n, hxn⟩ := hx
      rw [RingHom.mem_ker] at hxn ⊢
      have h₁ : (f x) ^ n = 0 := by
        rw [← map_pow]; exact hxn
      exact eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero h₁
    -- Step 1: For a closed immersion, the support of its kernel equals its range.
    -- (`support_ker` gives `closure (range)`; closed range collapses the closure.)
    have hi_range_eq : (i.ker.support : Set X) = Set.range i.base := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
      exact i.isClosedEmbedding.isClosed_range.closure_eq
    -- Step 2: `g.ker.support ⊆ i.ker.support` (set-theoretic, via `closure_minimal`).
    have hsup_set : (g.ker.support : Set X) ⊆ (i.ker.support : Set X) := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
      refine closure_minimal ?_ i.ker.support.isClosed
      exact hrange.trans hi_range_eq.ge
    -- Promote the set inclusion to the `Closeds X` ordering.
    have hsup : g.ker.support ≤ i.ker.support := hsup_set
    -- Step 3: `g.ker` is radical (because `Y` is reduced, so each `Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ U)` is reduced
    -- and the ring kernel is therefore radical, pointwise on affine opens).
    have hg_rad : g.ker.radical = g.ker := by
      refine le_antisymm ?_ Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical
      intro U
      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
      exact ker_isRadical (g.app U.1).hom
    -- Step 4: `i.ker` is radical (same argument; closed immersions are quasi-compact).
    have hi_rad : i.ker.radical = i.ker := by
      refine le_antisymm ?_ Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical
      intro U
      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
      exact ker_isRadical (i.app U.1).hom
    -- Step 5: thread the Galois connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support`.
    have hker : i.ker ≤ g.ker := by
      calc i.ker
          = i.ker.radical := hi_rad.symm
        _ = Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal i.ker.support :=
              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support.symm
        _ ≤ Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal g.ker.support :=
              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_antimono hsup
        _ = g.ker.radical := Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support
        _ = g.ker := hg_rad
    -- Step 6: feed the kernel inequality to Mathlib's `IsClosedImmersion.lift`.
    exact ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker, IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩

end IsClosedImmersion

end AlgebraicGeometry

end

# AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Cross01Substrate.lean

## Status

**RESOLVED axiom-clean — HARD BAR MET.** New file landed; Lane B Substrate 1
`IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` closed kernel-only (~80 LOC including
docs; ~50 LOC proof). Lake build green; `lean_verify` reports kernel-axioms
only (`propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound` — no project axioms, no
`sorryAx`).

## Substrate 1: `lift_iff_range_subset` (NEW; closed)

### Attempt 1

- **Approach:** 6-step Galois-connection recipe from `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md`
  §2, "Concrete project-side recipe":
  - **Step 1** (range = ker support): `Scheme.Hom.support_ker` (gives
    `f.ker.support = closure (range f)`) + `IsClosedEmbedding.isClosed_range.closure_eq`
    (since closed-immersions have closed range, the closure collapses).
  - **Step 2** (support inclusion): `closure_minimal` against
    `(i.ker.support : Set X)`'s `Closeds.isClosed`, threading
    `hrange.trans hi_range_eq.ge`. The `Set X`-level inclusion is then
    promoted to the `Closeds X` ordering by `SetLike` coercion
    (definitionally identical).
  - **Steps 3 + 4** (ker radical from reducedness): the kernel of a ring hom
    `f : R →+* S` into a reduced `S` is a radical ideal — built inline as a
    local `have ker_isRadical` (5 LOC: `obtain ⟨n, hxn⟩` → `map_pow` →
    `eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero`). Pushed through `Scheme.Hom.ker_apply` +
    `Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal` simp lemmas pointwise on affine opens.
  - **Step 5** (Galois-connection assembly): 5-step `calc` chain
    `i.ker = i.ker.radical = vanishingIdeal i.ker.support ≤
    vanishingIdeal g.ker.support = g.ker.radical = g.ker` via
    `Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support` (both directions) and
    `vanishingIdeal_antimono` (the antitone half of `IdealSheafData.gc`).
  - **Step 6** (apply Mathlib's `IsClosedImmersion.lift`): the existential
    witness is `⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker, IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED axiom-clean.
- **Bug encountered & fixed:** `Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical` takes an
  explicit `(I : IdealSheafData X)` argument; initial `refine le_antisymm ?_
  Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical` failed with "expected `g.ker ≤ g.ker.radical`,
  got `∀ I, I ≤ I.radical`". Fixed to `Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _`.
- **Key insights:**
  - **Closed-immersion ⇒ QuasiCompact** is automatic via the chain
    `IsClosedImmersion → IsAffineHom → QuasiCompact` (low-priority instances
    in `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Affine.lean:58` and
    `Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.lean:149`). No need to assume `[QuasiCompact i]`
    in the signature.
  - **`Scheme.Hom.support_ker`** is the cleaner equality
    (`f.ker.support = closure (range f)`) — Step 1 collapses the closure for
    closed immersions via `IsClosedEmbedding.isClosed_range.closure_eq`.
  - **Ring-kernel radicality from reduced codomain** doesn't need surjectivity
    — only the easy direction "S reduced ⟹ `(ker f).IsRadical`" is needed.
    Inlined as 5-LOC local `have` rather than promoting to a standalone
    helper (single use site).

## Forward direction note

The forward direction `(⇒)` of `lift_iff_range_subset` (range pushdown under
`g = h ≫ i`) closes in 4 lines via `rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩` + `rintro _ ⟨y, rfl⟩` +
`⟨h.base y, by simp [Scheme.Hom.comp_base]⟩`. No subtlety.

## Lemmas found / cited (all in Mathlib at b80f227)

- `Scheme.Hom.support_ker` (`IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:839`) — `f.ker.support = closure (range f)`.
- `Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support` (`IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:631`) —
  `vanishingIdeal I.support = I.radical`.
- `Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_antimono` (`IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:627`) —
  antitone half of `IdealSheafData.gc`.
- `Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal` simp lemma (auto-generated by
  `@[simps! ideal]` on `IdealSheafData.radical` def `IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:508-522`).
- `Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical` (`IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:534`) —
  `I ≤ I.radical`.
- `Scheme.Hom.ker_apply` (`IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:698`) —
  `f.ker.ideal U = RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom` under `[QuasiCompact f]`.
- `IsClosedImmersion.lift` (`Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.lean:206`) +
  `lift_fac` (line 211) — the kernel-inequality form Mathlib ships.
- `Scheme.Hom.isClosedEmbedding` alias (`Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.lean:48`) —
  the topological closed-embedding from `[IsClosedImmersion f]`.
- `eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero` (`Mathlib/Algebra/GroupWithZero/Basic.lean:195`) —
  for reduced rings, `x^n = 0 ⟹ x = 0`.

## Lemmas searched but ruled out

- `RingHom.ker_isRadical_iff_reduced_of_surjective`
  (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Nilpotent/Lemmas.lean:29`) requires surjectivity; not
  applicable. Inlined the 5-LOC easy direction directly.
- `Scheme.IdealSheafData.subschemeι` / `range_subschemeι`
  (`IdealSheaf/Subscheme.lean:472, 493`) — the analogist's recipe Step 1
  suggested routing through `toImage_imageι` + `range_subschemeι`. Cleaner
  to use `support_ker` directly (closed-immersion range is closed, so
  `closure (range i) = range i` immediately).

## Build verification

- `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` — empty (no errors, no
  warnings).
- `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_verify
  AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` →
  `{"axioms":["propext","Classical.choice","Quot.sound"],"warnings":[]}` —
  kernel axioms only.
- `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.Cross01Substrate` — ✔
- `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (the shim) — ✔ (after
  adding `import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.Cross01Substrate` to
  the shim so the file is reachable from the root).

## Side edit (registration, NOT proof)

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean` (the re-export shim): added the
  line `import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.Cross01Substrate` so the
  new file is reachable from `AlgebraicJacobian.lean` (necessary for `lake
  build` to compile it; without this, the file is orphaned). The shim's
  documented purpose is exactly to re-export from the four (now five)
  sub-stratum files of the iter-175 split; this is a pure import addition,
  no semantic change.

## Blueprint markers

- `chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`
  `thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset` — ready for `\leanok` on
  BOTH the statement and the proof. (Marker application is owned by the
  deterministic `sync_leanok` phase between prover and review; this section
  is informational for the sync agent.) The `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset}`
  cross-reference points to the now-landed declaration.

## Out of scope (deferred per plan-phase directive)

- **Substrate 2** (`gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain`, ~50–80 LOC)
  — owed iter-190 prover dispatch per the plan-phase commitment in
  `PROGRESS.md` iter-190 section. The blueprint
  `thm:gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain` block is already pinned in
  the chapter and ready to receive the body.
- **GmScaling.lean consumer applications**
  (`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` cocycle body, `gm_geomIrred`,
  `projGm_isReduced`) — owed iter-191–193 dispatch once Substrate 2 lands.

## Next-step recommendation

1. Plan agent: confirm Substrate 1 landed kernel-only; queue Substrate 2
   prover dispatch iter-190 per the recipe in
   `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` §2 "Substrate 2 (~50–80 LOC)".
2. Review agent: cross-check `\lean{...}` resolution in
   `chap-Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.html` once the blueprint
   web build runs; no `\mathlibok` correction needed (Mathlib has no
   such variant — verified iter-188 / iter-189 by analogist).
3. The Lane B watch in `PROGRESS.md` "Active monitors" section can
   record the substrate-close milestone iter-190 plan-phase.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).

10	/-!
11	# The identity component of the Picard scheme (A.3)
12	
13	This file is the **A.3** sub-build chapter for the project's positive-genus arm
14	of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It scaffolds the abstract identity-component
15	substrate for a `k`-group scheme locally of finite type and specialises it to
16	`G = Pic_{C/k}`, packaging:
17	
18	1. the open-and-closed subgroup-scheme structure of the identity component,
19	2. the degree map `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`,
20	3. the abelian-variety identification of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (the Jacobian variety
21	   of `C` when `C/k` is a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of
22	   positive genus).
23	
24	## Status (iter-185 NEW file-skeleton)
25	
26	This file is the **iter-185 IdentityComponent** file-skeleton (NEW lane,
27	blueprint-reviewer HARD GATE cleared for the iter-184 plan-phase chapter

94	/-! ## §1. The identity component of a group scheme — abstract substrate
95	
96	The identity component is an abstract feature of `k`-group schemes locally of
97	finite type. We package the definition + open-and-closed-subgroup-scheme
98	structure in the `GroupScheme` namespace so they are reusable outside the
99	Picard context.
100	
101	In this project, a "`k`-group scheme" is encoded as an
102	`Over (Spec (.of k))`-object `G` carrying a `[GrpObj G]` instance (the
103	group-object structure in the over-category). The locally-of-finite-type
104	hypothesis is the `[LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]` instance on the structural
105	morphism to the base.
106	
107	Blueprint references: `def:identity_component_group_scheme` +
108	`thm:identity_component_open_subgroup` (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`). -/
109	
110	namespace GroupScheme
111	
112	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, the set of
113	connected components is finite.
114	
115	Proof: the map `irreducibleComponents α → ConnectedComponents α` sending each
116	irreducible component `C` to the connected component of an arbitrary chosen
117	point of `C` is well-defined (because `C` is preconnected, hence contained in
118	a single connected component) and surjective (because every point lies in
119	some irreducible component, namely `irreducibleComponent x`, which is a
120	subset of `connectedComponent x`). The source is finite by
121	`TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`, so the
122	target is finite by `Finite.of_surjective`. -/
123	private lemma noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
124	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α] :
125	    Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := by
126	  classical
127	  have hfin : (irreducibleComponents α).Finite :=
128	    TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents
129	  haveI : Finite ↥(irreducibleComponents α) := hfin.to_subtype
130	  refine Finite.of_surjective
131	    (fun C : ↥(irreducibleComponents α) =>
132	      (ConnectedComponents.mk : α → ConnectedComponents α)
133	        ((C.property : Maximal IsIrreducible C.val).prop.nonempty.some)) ?_
134	  intro c
135	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := ConnectedComponents.surjective_coe c
136	  refine ⟨⟨irreducibleComponent x, irreducibleComponent_mem_irreducibleComponents x⟩, ?_⟩
137	  rw [ConnectedComponents.coe_eq_coe]
138	  refine (connectedComponent_eq ?_).symm
139	  exact irreducibleComponent_subset_connectedComponent
140	    (isIrreducible_irreducibleComponent (x := x)).nonempty.some_mem
141	
142	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, each
143	connected component is open.
144	
145	Proof: `ConnectedComponents α` is totally disconnected
146	(`ConnectedComponents.totallyDisconnectedSpace`), hence T1
147	(`TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space`), and finite by
148	`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`. A finite T1 space is discrete
149	(`Finite.instDiscreteTopology`), so the singleton
150	`{ConnectedComponents.mk x}` is open in the quotient. The preimage of this
151	singleton under the continuous quotient map `ConnectedComponents.mk` is
152	`connectedComponent x` (`connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`), which is
153	therefore open. -/
154	private lemma noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent
155	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α]
156	    (x : α) :
157	    IsOpen (connectedComponent x) := by
158	  haveI : Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
159	  haveI : DiscreteTopology (ConnectedComponents α) := inferInstance
160	  have h := (isOpen_discrete
161	    ({(ConnectedComponents.mk x : ConnectedComponents α)} : Set _)).preimage
162	    ConnectedComponents.continuous_coe
163	  rwa [connectedComponents_preimage_singleton] at h
164	
165	/-- Helper (iter-188): the **`LocallyConnectedSpace` instance** for the
166	underlying topological space of a `k`-scheme `G.left` whose structural
167	morphism `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec k` is locally of finite type.
168	
169	The substantive content is EGA I 6.1.9: a locally Noetherian topological
170	space has open connected components — equivalently, is locally connected.
171	Pushed through the chain: `Spec k` is Noetherian (a field has Noetherian
172	spectrum); `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom + IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k) ⟹
173	IsLocallyNoetherian G.left` (Mathlib's
174	`AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); and the
175	implication `IsLocallyNoetherian X ⟹ LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat`
176	is the iter-188 project-side helper (`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`
177	+ `noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent`).
178	
179	The classical proof: each point `y ∈ G.left` has an open affine
180	neighbourhood `W = Spec R` with `R` Noetherian, hence `|W|` is a Noetherian
181	topological space; in a Noetherian space, each `connectedComponent y` is
182	clopen (finite irreducible components ⟹ finite connected components ⟹
183	finite T1 quotient ⟹ discrete quotient ⟹ singletons clopen in quotient ⟹
184	preimages clopen). Pulled back along the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left`,
185	this gives an open connected neighbourhood of `y` inside any open `F`
186	containing `y`. -/
187	private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
188	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
189	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
190	    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
191	    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left := by
192	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian G.left := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian G.hom
193	  rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected]
194	  intro x F hF
195	  -- Extract an open neighbourhood `T ⊆ F` of `x`.
196	  obtain ⟨T, hTF, hT, hxT⟩ := mem_nhds_iff.mp hF
197	  -- Find an affine open `W ⊆ T` with `x ∈ W`.
198	  obtain ⟨W, hW, hxW, hWT⟩ :=
199	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := G.left) (U := ⟨T, hT⟩) hxT
200	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(G.left, W) :=
201	    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨W, hW⟩
202	  haveI hNoethW : TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace ↥W :=
203	    noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen W hW
204	  -- Inside `W` (Noetherian) the connected component of `x` is open; its image
205	  -- under the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left` is the connected open neighbourhood
206	  -- we want.
207	  let xW : ↥W := ⟨x, hxW⟩
208	  refine ⟨(Subtype.val : ↥W → G.left) '' connectedComponent xW, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
209	  · rintro _ ⟨z, _, rfl⟩
210	    exact hTF (hWT z.2)
211	  · exact W.isOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val _
212	      (noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent xW)
213	  · exact ⟨xW, mem_connectedComponent, rfl⟩
214	  · exact isConnected_connectedComponent.image _ continuous_subtype_val.continuousOn
215	
216	/-- The image of the identity section `e : Spec k → G` (well-defined as a
217	single point of `|G|` because `Spec k` is a topological singleton). -/
218	private noncomputable def identitySectionPoint
219	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] : G.left :=
220	  ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left.base :
221	      ↥(Spec (.of k)) → G.left) (default : Spec (.of k))
222	
223	/-- Helper (iter-186; iter-187 closed): the **clopen carrier `Set`** of
224	the identity component of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type,
225	packaged as a `G.left.Opens`. The carrier set is `connectedComponent x`
226	for `x = e(*)` the image of the identity section
227	(`identitySectionPoint G`); openness is `isOpen_connectedComponent` which
228	needs the `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` instance above
229	(EGA I 6.1.9). -/
230	private noncomputable def identityComponentCarrier {k : Type u} [Field k]
231	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
232	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
233	    G.left.Opens :=
234	  ⟨connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G), isOpen_connectedComponent⟩
235	
236	/-- The **identity component** `G^0` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
237	finite type.
238	
239	Encoded as a `k`-scheme: an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`, carrying the
240	intended substantive identity "this is the connected component of `|G|`
241	containing the identity section `e`, equipped with the open-subscheme
242	structure inherited from `G`". The associated open immersion
243	`IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` is the content of
244	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`.
245	
246	iter-186 body: built from the `identityComponentCarrier G` helper — the
247	open subscheme of `G` whose underlying topological space is the connected
248	component of `|G|` through the image of the identity section. The
249	structure morphism is the inherited `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`.
250	The substantive content (the actual carrier `Set`, plus its openness from
251	EGA I 6.1.9: locally Noetherian spaces have open connected components)
252	lives in the typed-sorry body of `identityComponentCarrier`. -/
253	noncomputable def IdentityComponent {k : Type u} [Field k]
254	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
255	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
256	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
257	  Over.mk ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
258	
259	/-- **The identity component is an open and closed subgroup scheme.**
260	
261	The bundled statement of Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3): the identity
262	component `G^0 = IdentityComponent G` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
263	finite type comes with a morphism `IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` (in
264	`Over (Spec (.of k))`) whose underlying scheme morphism is both an open
265	immersion and a closed immersion (i.e. the inclusion of a clopen
266	subscheme).
267	
268	The full Kleiman conclusion also packages the group-subscheme property (the
269	inclusion is a homomorphism of `k`-group schemes), finite-type-ness over `k`
270	(`LocallyOfFiniteType` + quasi-compactness), geometric irreducibility, and
271	base-change-commutation. Those refinements live as separate instances /
272	follow-up lemmas in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the clopen
273	open-immersion conclusion as a Nonempty-witness.
274	
275	iter-186 body: the inclusion morphism is `Over.homMk (identityComponentCarrier G).ι`,
276	with the over-category compatibility holding by definition of
277	`IdentityComponent G`. The `.left` of this morphism is
278	`(identityComponentCarrier G).ι`, an open immersion by the global
279	`Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι` instance. For the closed-immersion
280	half we apply `IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion` to the open immersion
281	and reduce to `IsClosed (↑(identityComponentCarrier G) : Set _)`. The
282	remaining inline `sorry` is the substantive content: the carrier of
283	`identityComponentCarrier G` is closed, by "closure of a connected subspace
284	is connected" applied to the connected component through the image of the
285	identity section. -/
286	theorem IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
287	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
288	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
289	    Nonempty {f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G //
290	        IsOpenImmersion f.left ∧ IsClosedImmersion f.left} := by
291	  -- Over-category compatibility for the underlying open immersion.
292	  have hcomp : (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
293	    simp [IdentityComponent]
294	  -- The over-morphism witness.
295	  let f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G :=
296	    Over.homMk (U := IdentityComponent G) (V := G) (identityComponentCarrier G).ι hcomp
297	  refine ⟨⟨f, ?_, ?_⟩⟩
298	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` (definitionally; `Over.homMk_left` simp).
299	    -- An open immersion by `Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι`.
300	    change IsOpenImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
301	    infer_instance
302	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` is a preimmersion (open immersion ⟹
303	    -- immersion ⟹ preimmersion). Its range is the carrier set (`Scheme.Opens.range_ι`),
304	    -- and closure of that connected subset is itself (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3):
305	    -- the connected component through the identity is closed in `|G|`).
306	    change IsClosedImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
307	    apply IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion
308	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
309	    -- The carrier set is `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`, which is
310	    -- closed by `isClopen_connectedComponent.1` (with the
311	    -- `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` instance providing the closedness
312	    -- half of EGA I 6.1.9: connected components of a locally Noetherian topological
313	    -- space are clopen).
314	    change IsClosed (connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G))
315	    exact isClopen_connectedComponent.1
316	
317	/-- **The identity component inclusion is a group-scheme homomorphism.**
318	
319	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (b): the clopen subscheme `G^0`
320	inherits a `k`-group-scheme structure from `G`, and the inclusion morphism
321	`G^0 ↪ G` (from `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`) is compatible
322	with the group laws on source and target. The statement-level pin asserts
323	the existence of the inherited `GrpObj` structure; the compatibility of
324	the inclusion with the group operations is the substantive content of
325	Kleiman's argument (the product `G^0 ×_k G^0` is connected by
326	EGA IV₂ 4.5.8; the group-multiplication map sends this connected subset
327	containing the identity into the connected component `G^0`).
328	
329	The full statement (existence of `GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)` *together*
330	with the homomorphism-compatibility of the inclusion in the relevant
331	`Hom`-form) lives in iter-188+. -/
332	theorem IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism {k : Type u} [Field k]
333	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
334	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
335	    Nonempty (GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)) :=
336	  sorry
337	
338	/-- **The identity component is of finite type and geometrically irreducible.**
339	
340	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (c): the open subgroup `G^0` of
341	a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type is itself
342	locally-of-finite-type-plus-quasi-compact (i.e., of finite type) over `k`,
343	and is geometrically irreducible. The proof reduces (after base change to
344	`\bar k`) to picking a nonempty smooth affine open subset `U ⊆ G^0`; the
345	translates `hg⁻¹U` give an open cover of `G^0` by smooth, hence irreducible,
346	neighbourhoods, so `G^0` is locally irreducible at every closed point; with
347	connectedness this gives irreducibility globally (EGA I 6.1.10). The image
348	`α(U × U) = G^0` is the image of the affine, hence quasi-compact, scheme
349	`U × U`, so `G^0` is quasi-compact. -/
350	theorem IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible
351	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
352	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
353	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
354	    LocallyOfFiniteType (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
355	      QuasiCompact (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
356	      GeometricallyIrreducible (IdentityComponent G).hom :=
357	  sorry
358	
359	/-- **Formation of the identity component commutes with base change.**
360	
361	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (d): for any field extension
362	`K/k`, the natural comparison map `(G^0)_K → (G_K)^0` is an isomorphism of
363	`K`-schemes. The proof: `G^0` is geometrically connected as a connected
364	scheme with a `k`-rational point (EGA IV₂ 4.5.14), so its base change
365	`(G^0)_K` remains connected; it is also open and closed in `G_K`, so
366	coincides with the identity component `(G_K)^0`.
367	
368	The statement-level pin asserts existence of: a `K`-group-scheme structure
369	on the base change `G ×_{Spec k} Spec K` (with appropriate
370	locally-of-finite-type instance), and an isomorphism on underlying schemes
371	identifying the two iterated constructions. The iso witness is a typed
372	sorry pending iter-188+ implementation; the substantive `GrpObj` is
373	provided by `Scheme.GrpObjAsOverPullback` and is propagated through. -/
374	theorem IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
375	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
376	    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra k K] :
377	    let φ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) :=
378	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))
379	    let G_K : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of K)) :=
380	      Over.mk (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ)
381	    Nonempty (Σ' (_grpInst : GrpObj G_K)
382	                 (_locFTInst : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom),
383	      (IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅
384	        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ) :=
385	  sorry
386	
387	end GroupScheme

389	/-! ## §2. The identity component of the Picard scheme
390	
391	We specialise the abstract identity-component substrate to
392	`G = PicScheme C`, the Picard scheme of a smooth proper geometrically
393	integral curve `C/k` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`).
394	
395	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_zero_subscheme` (Kleiman §5 opening + Prp.
396	`prp:pic0`). -/
397	
398	namespace Scheme
399	
400	/-- The **identity component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.
401	
402	Defined as the identity component
403	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` of the Picard scheme
404	`Pic_{C/k}` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`,
405	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`). By
406	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`, `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an
407	open and closed subgroup scheme of `Pic_{C/k}` of finite type over `k`,
408	geometrically irreducible, and its formation commutes with extension of the
409	base field.
410	
411	iter-186+: the body unwinds to `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`
412	once the `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` instance lands on
413	`PicScheme C` (it follows from the Kleiman §4 structure: `Pic_{C/k}` is
414	locally of finite type as the disjoint union of open quasi-projective
415	`k`-subschemes). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
416	noncomputable def Pic0Scheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
417	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
418	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
419	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
420	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
421	  sorry
422	
423	/-! ## §3. The degree map
424	
425	The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
426	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
427	`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
428	coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
429	relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
430	**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
431	
432	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/
433	
434	namespace PicScheme
435	
436	/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
437	
438	Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
439	`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
440	Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
441	to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
442	`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
443	coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
444	the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
445	relative Picard functor).
446	
447	The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
448	`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
449	group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
450	lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.
451	
452	iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
453	`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
454	Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
455	the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
456	`sorry`. -/
457	noncomputable def degree {k : Type u} [Field k]
458	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
459	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
460	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
461	    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
462	  sorry
463	
464	end PicScheme
465	
466	/-! ## §4. `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety
467	
468	The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
469	abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
470	this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
471	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
472	threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by
473	`AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP`.
474	
475	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
476	Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/
477	
478	namespace Pic0Scheme
479	
480	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**
481	
482	For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
483	over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
484	is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
485	irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
486	Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)
487	
488	The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
489	[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
490	`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.
491	
492	Dimension `g = g(C)`: not stated separately at the file-skeleton level
493	(it requires `dim_k (Pic0Scheme C).left = AlgebraicGeometry.genus C`, which
494	involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
495	follow-up.
496	
497	iter-186+: the body assembles the four conjuncts from
498	- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of
499	  `PicScheme C`),
500	- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹
501	  `Pic⁰_{X/k}` quasi-projective; upgrade to projective when geom. normal,
502	  which holds for smooth proper curves of positive genus),
503	- Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Ex.~`ex:jac` (smoothness of `Pic_{X/k}` at the
504	  identity for smooth proper curves; hence smooth of dimension
505	  `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)` everywhere by uniform smoothness on the open
506	  identity component).
507	For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
508	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
509	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
510	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
511	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
512	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
513	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
514	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
515	  sorry
516	
517	/-- **Dimension of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals the genus of `C`.**
518	
519	Milne~§III.1, Rmk.~1.4(e): "The dimension of `J` is the genus of `C`".
520	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k` of genus
521	`g = g(C)`, the topological Krull dimension of the underlying scheme of
522	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals `g(C)`. By Kleiman~§5 Cor.~`cor:sm`, the inequality
523	`dim Pic_{C/k} ≤ dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` is always an equality at points where
524	`Pic_{C/k}` is smooth, and for smooth proper curves
525	(`SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom`) the identity component is smooth
526	(Kleiman~§5 Ex.~`ex:jac`), so the dimension equals `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)`
527	by `def:genus`. -/
528	theorem finrank_eq_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
529	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
530	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
531	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
532	    topologicalKrullDim (Pic0Scheme C).left = (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : WithBot ℕ∞) :=
533	  sorry
534	
535	/-- **`k`-points of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` are the kernel of the degree map.**
536	
537	Milne~§III.1, p.~88: `Pic⁰(C)` is the group of isomorphism classes of
538	invertible sheaves of degree zero on `C`. For a smooth proper geometrically
539	integral curve `C/k`, a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` lies in the image of
540	the inclusion `Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}` (the inclusion of
541	`def:pic_zero_subscheme`, packaged here via the existence of the
542	inclusion morphism) if and only if `degree C λ = 0`.
543	
544	The statement-level pin packages two pieces: existence of the inclusion
545	morphism `Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶ Pic_{C/k}` (extracted from
546	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` once `PicScheme C` has the
547	`GrpObj` + `LocallyOfFiniteType` instances), together with the
548	characterisation of `k`-points factoring through it as those with degree
549	zero. -/
550	theorem kPoints_iff_kerDegree {k : Type u} [Field k]
551	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
552	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
553	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
554	    Nonempty (Σ' (inc : (Pic0Scheme C).left ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
555	      ∀ (lambda : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
556	        (∃ mu : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left, mu ≫ inc = lambda) ↔
557	          PicScheme.degree C lambda = 0) :=
558	  sorry
559	
560	end Pic0Scheme
561	
562	end Scheme
563	
564	end AlgebraicGeometry

2	\label{chap:Picard_IdentityComponent}

11	% (\cref{def:identity_component_group_scheme} +

13	% the Lean target
14	% \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.IdentityComponent} does not yet
15	% exist in Mathlib.
16	%
17	% NOTE: future split candidate when a second consumer materialises. The
18	% abstract identity-component substrate is kept in this chapter (rather
19	% than split off into a separate

22	% revisit the split.
23	
24	% NOTE (planner directive iter-184). The Lean target file
