110	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
111	
112	/-! ## §1. The line bundle of a closed point on a smooth proper curve
113	
114	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` and a closed
115	point `P ∈ C`, the local ring `𝒪_{C,P}` is a DVR with maximal ideal
116	generated by a uniformiser `f_P`. Hartshorne's construction `ℒ(D)`
117	(II §6 p. 144) applied to the Cartier divisor `[P]` (locally cut out by
118	`f_P` near `P`, by `1` elsewhere) produces the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)`:
119	the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)`
120	generated locally by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on `C \ {P}`. -/
121	
122	namespace Scheme
123	
124	/-! ### Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C` carrier (iter-183 Lane A scaffold)
125	
126	The substantive iter-183 contribution: a concrete per-open `Set`-valued
127	carrier of the line bundle `𝒪_C(P)`, realised directly as Hartshorne's
128	subsheaf of the function-field constant sheaf `K_C` (Hartshorne II §6
129	p. 144; analogist `ocofp-sheaf-internalhom.md` Decision 3
130	`ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB`).
131	
132	A section of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U` is a rational function `f ∈ K(C)`
133	satisfying the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor
134	`Q ≠ P` with `Q.point ∈ U` (regularity on the complement of `P` inside
135	`U`) and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
136	The construction is independent of the choice of uniformiser at `P`:
137	any two uniformisers differ by a unit, so the order-`≥ −1` condition at
138	`P` is intrinsic.
139	
140	iter-184+ will upgrade `carrierSet` to a `Submodule kbar K(C)` via the
141	closure proofs (zero / addition / kbar-scalar), bundle the result as a
142	presheaf functor (identity-on-`K(C)` restrictions, monotone in `U` via
143	`carrierSet_mono`), and discharge the sheaf property via gluing-by-
144	stalks (stalk-locality of the order conditions at each prime divisor). -/
145	
146	/-- **Carrier set** of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U : (Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`: the
147	set of rational functions `f ∈ K(C)` satisfying the order conditions
148	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q` with `Q.point ∈ U.unop`,
149	`Q.point ≠ P`, and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U.unop`.
150	
151	iter-183 Lane A landed this as a concrete substantive `Set` definition;
152	no sorry. -/
153	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet
154	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
155	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
156	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
157	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
158	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
159	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
160	    Set C.left.functionField := by
161	  let Phat : C.left.PrimeDivisor := ⟨P, hPcoh⟩
162	  haveI := Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instKrullDimLEStalk
163	    (X := C.left) Phat
164	  exact { f | (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ∈ U.unop.1 → Q.point ≠ P →
165	              0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
166	              (P ∈ U.unop.1 → (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Phat f) }
167	
168	/-- **Monotonicity of `carrierSet` in `U`**: when `V.unop ⊆ U.unop` (i.e.
169	the open `V` is contained in the open `U`), the carrier on `U` is
170	INCLUDED in the carrier on `V` (the order conditions over the smaller
171	open `V` involve fewer prime divisors, hence are easier to satisfy).
172	
173	This is the substantive monotonicity that drives the (identity-on-`K(C)`)
174	restriction map of the would-be `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.presheaf` functor:
175	in `(Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`, an arrow `U ⟶ V` corresponds to `V.unop ⊆ U.unop`,
176	and the restriction map `carrierSet U → carrierSet V` is the inclusion
177	delivered by this lemma.
178	
179	iter-183 Lane A landed this as the substantive monotonicity proof; no
180	sorry. -/
181	private lemma lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet_mono
182	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
183	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
184	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
185	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
186	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
187	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ}
188	    (hUV : V.unop.1 ⊆ U.unop.1) :
189	    lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh U
190	      ⊆ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh V := by
191	  intro f hf
192	  refine ⟨fun Q hQV hQP => hf.1 Q (hUV hQV) hQP, fun hPV => hf.2 (hUV hPV)⟩
193	
194	/-- **Nonempty top-open**: for an integral scheme `X`, the top open is
195	nonempty (its carrier is `Set.univ` and `X` itself is nonempty since
196	`IsIntegral X ⟹ IrreducibleSpace X ⟹ Nonempty X`). Supplied as an
197	instance so that the standard Mathlib `Algebra Γ(X, U) X.functionField`
198	instance fires at `U = ⊤`. -/
199	instance lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instNonemptyTopOpen
200	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] :
201	    Nonempty ((⊤ : X.Opens) : Scheme) :=
202	  (Scheme.Opens.nonempty_iff (U := (⊤ : X.Opens))).mpr
203	    ⟨(inferInstance : Nonempty X).some, Set.mem_univ _⟩
204	
205	/-- **`kbar`-algebra structure on the function field `K(C)`**. The
206	project's `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraSection` instance gives
207	`Algebra kbar Γ(C.left, ⊤)`, and Mathlib's standard
208	`AlgebraicGeometry.instAlgebraCarrierObjOppositeOpens...` gives
209	`Algebra Γ(C.left, ⊤) K(C)` via `germToFunctionField`. Composing the
210	two algebra maps via `RingHom.toAlgebra` produces the desired
211	`Algebra kbar K(C)` instance, which is needed for `Submodule kbar K(C)`
212	to type-check. -/
213	noncomputable instance lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instAlgebraKbarFunctionField
214	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
215	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsIntegral C.left] :
216	    Algebra kbar C.left.functionField := by
217	  haveI : Nonempty (⊤ : C.left.Opens) :=
218	    (AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.nonempty_iff
219	      (X := C.left) (U := (⊤ : C.left.Opens))).mpr
220	      ⟨(inferInstance : Nonempty C.left).some, Set.mem_univ _⟩
221	  exact RingHom.toAlgebra
222	    ((Scheme.germToFunctionField C.left (⊤ : C.left.Opens)).hom.comp
223	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C
224	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).hom)
225	
226	/-- **Carrier submodule** of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U`: upgrade of
227	`carrierSet U` to a `Submodule kbar K(C)` via the three closure proofs
228	(`0`, `+`, `kbar • _`). The closure proofs rest on the DVR-shipped
229	`Ring.ordFrac_add` (non-archimedean inequality on the discrete valuation
230	of a regular-in-codim-1 stalk; iter-186 Step 1 upgraded the carrier class
231	so this is invocable) and `Ring.ordFrac_of_isUnit` (the scalar from
232	`kbar` becomes a unit in the stalk, preserving the order under scalar
233	multiplication).
234	
235	iter-186 Step 2 (per analogist `ocofp-carrierset-submodule-api.md`
236	Decision 2): structural skeleton with the load-bearing Mathlib lemmas in
237	place; bookkeeping `sorry`s remain for the prover phase. -/
238	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule
239	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
240	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
241	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
242	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
243	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
244	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
245	    Submodule kbar C.left.functionField where
246	  carrier := lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh U
247	  zero_mem' := by
248	    -- `order Y 0 = WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0`;
249	    -- both `0 ≤ 0` and `-1 ≤ 0` hold trivially.
250	    refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩
251	    · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
252	    · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
253	  add_mem' := by
254	    -- Case-split on `f + g = 0` (trivial via `WithZero.log_zero`), else
255	    -- apply `Ring.ordFrac_add` (DVR-shipped, Step 1 unlocks) and
256	    -- `WithZero.log` monotonicity on the nonzero part.
257	    rintro a b ⟨ha₁, ha₂⟩ ⟨hb₁, hb₂⟩
258	    have hMNZ : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (x : C.left.functionField),
259	        x ≠ 0 → Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point) x ≠ 0 := by
260	      intro Q x hx; simp [hx]
261	    have key : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ),
262	        n ≤ 0 → n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q a →
263	          n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q b →
264	          n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (a + b) := by
265	      intro Q n hn haₚ hbₚ
266	      by_cases hab : a + b = 0
267	      · simpa [hab, Scheme.RationalMap.order] using hn
268	      by_cases hae : a = 0
269	      · rw [hae, zero_add]; exact hbₚ
270	      by_cases hbe : b = 0
271	      · rw [hbe, add_zero]; exact haₚ
272	      set R := C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point
273	      have hoa : Ring.ordFrac R a ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q a hae
274	      have hob : Ring.ordFrac R b ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q b hbe
275	      have hoab : Ring.ordFrac R (a + b) ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q _ hab
276	      have hmin : Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b ≤ Ring.ordFrac R (a + b) :=
277	        Ring.ordFrac_add (R := R) a b hab
278	      have hlog : (Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b).log ≤
279	          Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (a + b) := by
280	        rcases min_cases (Ring.ordFrac R a) (Ring.ordFrac R b) with
281	            ⟨heq, _⟩ | ⟨heq, _⟩
282	        · rw [heq]; exact (WithZero.log_le_log hoa hoab).mpr (heq ▸ hmin)
283	        · rw [heq]; exact (WithZero.log_le_log hob hoab).mpr (heq ▸ hmin)
284	      have hminbd : n ≤ (Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b).log := by
285	        rcases min_cases (Ring.ordFrac R a) (Ring.ordFrac R b) with
286	            ⟨heq, _⟩ | ⟨heq, _⟩
287	        · rw [heq]; exact haₚ
288	        · rw [heq]; exact hbₚ
289	      linarith
290	    refine ⟨fun Q hQU hQP => key Q 0 le_rfl (ha₁ Q hQU hQP) (hb₁ Q hQU hQP),
291	      fun hPU => key ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ (-1) (by norm_num) (ha₂ hPU) (hb₂ hPU)⟩
292	  smul_mem' := by
293	    -- For `c : kbar`, the scalar action `c • f` on `K(C)` factors through
294	    -- `algebraMap kbar K(C)`. For `c ≠ 0`, `algebraMap kbar K(C) c` lifts to
295	    -- a nonzero `β` in every stalk via the germ map; `Ring.ordFrac_ge_one_of_ne_zero`
296	    -- on `β` then gives `order Q (algebraMap c) ≥ 0`, hence `c•x ∈ carrierSet`.
297	    intro c x ⟨hx₁, hx₂⟩
298	    rcases eq_or_ne c 0 with rfl | hc
299	    · -- `c = 0` ⇒ `0 • x = 0` ∈ carrierSet via `zero_mem'`.
300	      simp only [zero_smul]
301	      refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩ <;> simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
302	    rcases eq_or_ne x 0 with rfl | hx_ne
303	    · -- `x = 0` ⇒ `c • 0 = 0` ∈ carrierSet.
304	      simp only [smul_zero]
305	      refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩ <;> simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
306	    -- Both `c ≠ 0` and `x ≠ 0`: compute `order Q (c•x)` via multiplicativity.
307	    have hsmul : c • x = (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) * x :=
308	      Algebra.smul_def c x
309	    have hαne : (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) ≠ 0 := by
310	      have hinj := FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective kbar C.left.functionField
311	      simpa using hinj.ne_iff.mpr hc
312	    have hMNZ : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (y : C.left.functionField),
313	        y ≠ 0 → Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point) y ≠ 0 := by
314	      intro Q y hy; simp [hy]
315	    have key_alpha_ge : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor),
316	        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q
317	              (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) := by
318	      intro Q
319	      set R := C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point
320	      let β : R := (C.left.presheaf.germ (⊤ : C.left.Opens) Q.point trivial).hom
321	        ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C
322	            (Opposite.op (⊤ : C.left.Opens))).hom c)
323	      have hα_eq : (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) =
324	          algebraMap R C.left.functionField β := by
325	        change ((Scheme.germToFunctionField C.left (⊤ : C.left.Opens)).hom
326	            ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C
327	                (Opposite.op (⊤ : C.left.Opens))).hom c)) =
328	            (C.left.presheaf.stalkSpecializes
329	              ((genericPoint_spec C.left).specializes trivial)).hom β
330	        rw [← TopCat.Presheaf.germ_stalkSpecializes_apply
331	          (h := (genericPoint_spec C.left).specializes trivial)]
332	      have hβne : β ≠ 0 := by
333	        intro hzero; apply hαne; rw [hα_eq, hzero, map_zero]
334	      have hαord_ne : Ring.ordFrac R
335	          (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q _ hαne
336	      have hge : (1 : WithZero (Multiplicative ℤ)) ≤
337	          Ring.ordFrac R (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) := by
338	        rw [hα_eq]; exact Ring.ordFrac_ge_one_of_ne_zero hβne
339	      unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
340	      rw [show (0 : ℤ) = WithZero.log (1 : WithZero (Multiplicative ℤ)) by simp]
341	      exact (WithZero.log_le_log (by norm_num) hαord_ne).mpr hge
342	    have key : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ),
343	        n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q x →
344	        n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (c • x) := by
345	      intro Q n hxn
346	      rw [hsmul]
347	      set R := C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point
348	      have hαnez := hMNZ Q _ hαne
349	      have hxnez := hMNZ Q x hx_ne
350	      unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
351	      rw [map_mul, WithZero.log_mul hαnez hxnez]
352	      have := key_alpha_ge Q
353	      unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order at this hxn
354	      linarith
355	    refine ⟨fun Q hQU hQP => key Q 0 (hx₁ Q hQU hQP),
356	      fun hPU => key ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ (-1) (hx₂ hPU)⟩
357	
358	/-- **Bot-trivialization submodule** (iter-188 Step S1 of the sheaf-property
359	close): the per-open submodule of `K(C)` that equals `⊥` at `U = ⊥` and
360	`⊤` otherwise. The factor `carrierSubmodule ⊓ trivAtBot` enforces the
361	correct sheaf-at-`⊥` semantics (`F(⊥) = 0`), which is required for the
362	`Opens.grothendieckTopology` sheaf condition (the empty cover of `⊥`
363	forces `F(⊥) = 0`). -/
364	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.trivAtBot
365	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
366	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
367	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
368	    Submodule kbar C.left.functionField where
369	  carrier := {f | U.unop ≠ (⊥ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) ∨ f = 0}
370	  zero_mem' := Or.inr rfl
371	  add_mem' := by
372	    rintro a b (ha | ha) (hb | hb)
373	    · exact Or.inl ha
374	    · exact Or.inl ha
375	    · exact Or.inl hb
376	    · right; rw [ha, hb]; ring
377	  smul_mem' := by
378	    rintro c x (hx | hx)
379	    · exact Or.inl hx
380	    · right; rw [hx]; simp
381	
382	/-- **Sheaf-corrected carrier submodule** (iter-188 Step S2 of the sheaf-
383	property close): the per-open submodule `carrierSubmodule U ⊓ trivAtBot U`.
384	At `U ≠ ⊥` this equals `carrierSubmodule U` (since `trivAtBot U = ⊤`);
385	at `U = ⊥` this equals `⊥` (since `trivAtBot ⊥ = ⊥`). The latter is the
386	correct value for a sheaf in the `Opens.grothendieckTopology`, where the
387	empty cover of `⊥` forces `F(⊥) = 0`. -/
388	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf
389	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
390	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
391	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
392	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
393	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
394	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
395	    Submodule kbar C.left.functionField :=
396	  lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule P hPcoh U ⊓
397	    lineBundleAtClosedPoint.trivAtBot (C := C) U
398	
399	/-- **Monotonicity of `carrierSubmoduleSheaf` in `U` when the target `V`
400	is non-empty**: when `V.unop ⊆ U.unop` and `V.unop ≠ ⊥`, the carrier on
401	`U` is included in the carrier on `V`. The non-empty hypothesis is
402	necessary: if `V = ⊥`, the inclusion can fail because nonzero `f`'s in
403	`carrierSubmoduleSheaf U` need not satisfy the trivAtBot-at-⊥ condition
404	(only `0` does). -/
405	private lemma lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf_le
406	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
407	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
408	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
409	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]

423	an open `U` is a function `g : U.unop → K(C)` that is constant with value
424	`f ∈ carrierSubmoduleSheaf U`.

426	This Subfunctor packages the carrier conditions uniformly:
427	- At `U ≠ ⊥`: a section is a constant function valued in
428	  `carrierSubmoduleSheaf U = carrierSubmodule U` (so the order conditions hold).
429	- At `U = ⊥`: the unique empty function (witnessed by `f = 0 ∈ ⊥`).

434	every section of `presheafToType K(C)` whose sieve-of-section is covering already
435	lies in the Subfunctor. On the irreducible scheme `C.left`, this stalk-locality
436	holds because any two non-empty opens intersect, forcing constant-function gluing

797	/-! ## §2. Global sections as the Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`
798	
799	Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7 identifies the global sections of
800	`ℒ(D_0)` with the rational functions `f ∈ K(X)^×` satisfying
801	`div(f) ≥ −D_0` (plus the zero section). Specialised to `D_0 = [P]` on a
802	curve, the condition `div(f) + [P] ≥ 0` rewrites coordinate-wise as
803	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`. -/
804	
805	/-- **Forward direction of `globalSections_iff` (Hartshorne II.7.7(b)).**

3	\chapter{The line bundle \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) and its global sections (RR.3)}
4	\label{chap:RiemannRoch_OCofP}
5	
6	% SOURCE: Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, II.6, p.~144 (definition
7	% of \(\mathscr L(D)\)); II.7, Proposition~7.7, p.~157 (effective divisors
8	% as divisors of zeros of global sections); IV.1, pp.~294--297
9	% (Riemann--Roch, Lemma~1.2, Examples~1.3.3--1.3.5)
10	% (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages 161,
11	% 174, 311--314). The verbatim quotes pasted in the declaration blocks
12	% below were copied character-by-character from the rendered page images
13	% of the scanned PDF (no text layer is present).
14	
15	\section{Setup and motivation}
16	
17	This chapter is the third of four sub-build chapters \texttt{RR.1}--\texttt{RR.4}
18	that close the project's headline \emph{RR bridge}
19	\[
20	  \mathtt{genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}
21	  \,\colon\,
22	  \text{a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with
23	  \(g(C) = 0\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).}
24	\]
25	The bridge as proved in Hartshorne (Example~IV.1.3.5) chains together:
26	the Weil-divisor group \(\mathrm{Div}(C)\), the degree map $\deg \colon \mathrm{Div}(C)
27	\to \mathbb Z\(, and the divisor \)[P]$ of a closed point
28	(\texttt{RR.1}, REF); the Riemann--Roch
29	dimension formula in genus~\(0\)
30	\[
31	  \ell(D) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1 \qquad \text{for } \deg(D) \geq 0
32	\]
33	via the Euler-characteristic identity
34	(\texttt{RR.2}, REF); the global-sections
35	calculation \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2\) on a smooth
36	proper genus-\(0\) curve (this chapter, \texttt{RR.3}); the
37	``rational curve \(\Rightarrow \cong \mathbb P^1\)'' classification via
38	\texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} (\texttt{RR.4}).
39	
40	The contribution of \texttt{RR.3} is twofold. First, it constructs the
41	invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) associated to a closed point \(P \in C\)
42	on a smooth proper curve, identifies its \(\bar k\)-vector space of global
43	sections with the \emph{Riemann--Roch space}
44	\[
45	  L([P]) \;=\;
46	  \bigl\{f \in K(C)^{\times} \;\big|\; \div(f) + [P] \geq 0\bigr\}
47	  \,\cup\, \{0\},
48	\]
49	and computes its dimension as \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2\)
50	in the genus-\(0\) specialisation. Second, it deduces from the
51	two-dimensionality that \(C\) admits a non-constant rational function \(f\)
52	having at most a simple pole at \(P\) and no other poles. This rational
53	function is the seed for \texttt{RR.4}: the pair $(1, f) \in
54	H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))^{\oplus 2}$ generates the sheaf and, via
55	\texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections}, produces the morphism
56	\(C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) whose degree-\(1\) identification is the
57	content of \texttt{RR.4}.
58	
59	% NOTE (Direct cohomological vanishing vs. Serre duality — iter-174
60	% sub-phase choice). The dimension computation
61	% \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2\) would, in Hartshorne's
62	% original presentation, route through Serre duality
63	% \(H^1(C, \mathcal L) \cong H^0(C, \omega_C \otimes \mathcal L^{-1})^*\)
64	% to vanish \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) from the degree count
65	% \(\deg(K_C - [P]) = 2g - 2 - 1 = -3 < 0\). Mathlib (snapshot
66	% \texttt{b80f227}) ships no dualizing sheaf \(\omega_X\) on a scheme and
67	% no Serre duality theorem. The genus-\(0\) specialisation does \emph{not}
68	% require Serre duality: the vanishing \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 0\) is
69	% delivered by the long exact sequence of the structural short exact
70	% sequence
71	% \[
72	%   0 \to \mathcal O_C \to \mathcal O_C(P) \to k(P) \to 0
73	% \]
74	% together with the genus-\(0\) hypothesis
75	% \(\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = g = 0\) and the skyscraper
76	% identity \(H^1(C, k(P)) = 0\). The chapter therefore uses the direct
77	% cohomological vanishing route exclusively; Serre duality is left for
78	% the post-RR-bridge follow-up chapter that handles the general-\(g\)
79	% statement.
80	
81	\paragraph{Standing hypotheses.} Throughout this chapter, \(\bar k\) is an
82	algebraically closed field and \(C\) is a smooth proper geometrically
83	irreducible curve over \(\bar k\). Concretely, the Lean signatures
84	(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean}) carry the same
85	typeclass package as the rest of the project's curve API:
86	\begin{quote}
87	  \texttt{\{kbar : Type u\} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]\\
88	   (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]\\
89	   \phantom{}\quad[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]\\
90	   \phantom{}\quad[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]}
91	\end{quote}
92	The genus \(g(C)\) is the one defined in REF of
93	\texttt{Genus.tex}; the divisor \([P]\) of a closed point is the one of
94	REF of \texttt{RiemannRoch\_WeilDivisor.tex};
95	the principal divisor \(\div(f)\) of a nonzero rational function and the
96	order \(\ord_Q(f)\) at a prime divisor are the ones of
97	REF and REF.

110	\begin{definition}
111	\leanok
112	[Presheaf carrier for the line bundle of a closed point]
113	  \label{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierPresheaf}
114	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf}
115	  For each open \(U \subseteq C\), the \(\bar k\)-submodule
116	  \(\mathrm{carrierSubmodule}_P(U) \subset K(C)\) of section candidates
117	  satisfying the order conditions ``\(\mathrm{ord}_Q f \ge 0\) for all
118	  prime divisors \(Q \in U\) other than \(P\), and additionally
119	  \(\mathrm{ord}_P f \ge -1\) when \(P \in U\)'' assembles into a
120	  \(\bar k\)-linear functor
121	  \[
122	    \texttt{carrierPresheaf}_P : (\Opens\, |C|)^{\op}
123	    \longrightarrow \ModuleCat_{\bar k},
124	    \qquad U \longmapsto \mathrm{carrierSubmodule}_P(U),
125	  \]
126	  with restriction \(V \subseteq U \mapsto \texttt{Submodule.inclusion}\,
127	  (\texttt{carrierSet\_mono})\) on each pair. The functor laws follow from
128	  the antitone monotonicity \(U \mapsto \mathrm{carrierSet}_P(U)\) (witness
129	  \(\texttt{carrierSet\_mono}\) at L181-192 of the Lean file) by
130	  \(\texttt{ext}\,\langle x, \_\rangle\); \texttt{rfl}.

133	\begin{lemma}
134	\leanok
135	[Sheaf property of the carrier presheaf]
136	  \label{lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierPresheaf_isSheaf}
137	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf_isSheaf}
138	  \uses{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierPresheaf,
139	        def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierSubmoduleSheaf}

141	  carrier-refinement of \cref{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierSubmoduleSheaf})

143	  the locality + gluing conditions for \(\ModuleCat_{\bar k}\)-valued sections
144	  reduce (via \(\texttt{isSheaf\_iff\_isSheaf\_forget}\)) to the sheaf
145	  condition on the underlying Set-valued functor: each
146	  \(\mathrm{carrierSubmoduleSheaf}_P(U)\) is a subset of the constant

154	\begin{definition}
155	[Carrier-submodule refinement enforcing \(F(\bot)=0\)]
156	  \label{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierSubmoduleSheaf}
157	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf}
158	  \uses{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierPresheaf}
159	  Iter-188 Lane A discovery: the raw \(\mathrm{carrierSubmodule}_P\) of
160	  \cref{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierPresheaf} satisfies
161	  \(\mathrm{carrierSubmodule}_P(\bot) = K(C)\) (since the order conditions
162	  are vacuously satisfied on the empty open). This violates the sheaf
163	  axiom on the Grothendieck topology of opens: the empty sieve covers
164	  \(\bot\), forcing \(F(\bot) =\) terminal \(= 0\) on
165	  \(\ModuleCat_{\bar k}\)-valued functors.
166	  The refinement
167	  \[
168	    \mathrm{carrierSubmoduleSheaf}_P(U) \;:=\;
169	    \mathrm{carrierSubmodule}_P(U) \;\sqcap\; \mathrm{trivAtBot}\,U,
170	    \qquad \mathrm{trivAtBot}\,U \;:=\;
171	    \{\, f \;\mid\; U \ne \bot \,\lor\, f = 0 \,\}.
172	  \]
173	  forces \(F(\bot) = \bot\) (intersection annihilates) while preserving
174	  \(F(U)\) on non-empty \(U\) (since then \(\mathrm{trivAtBot}\,U = \top\)).
175	  The case-based restriction map (zero target on \(\bot\); inclusion
176	  otherwise) gives a well-formed sheaf-condition substrate consumed by
177	  \cref{lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierPresheaf_isSheaf}. Downstream
178	  consumers \texttt{lineBundleAtClosedPoint}, \texttt{toFunctionField},
179	  \texttt{globalSections\_iff\_*} are updated to supply the
180	  \texttt{trivAtBot} witness at \(\op\,\top\) via the \texttt{htop\_ne\_bot}
181	  helper.
182	\end{definition}

184	\begin{definition}
185	
186	
187	\leanok
188	[Line bundle of a closed point]
189	  \label{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint}
190	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint}
191	  \uses{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierPresheaf, lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierPresheaf_isSheaf}
192	  
193	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, p.~144 (definition of the invertible
194	  % sheaf \(\mathscr L(D)\) associated to a Cartier divisor) (read from
195	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 161).
196	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Definition. Let \(D\) be a Cartier divisor on a scheme
197	  % \(X\), represented by \(\{(U_i, f_i)\}\) as above. We define a subsheaf
198	  % \(\mathscr L(D)\) of the sheaf of total quotient rings \(\mathscr K\) by
199	  % taking \(\mathscr L(D)\) to be the sub-\(\mathscr O_X\)-module of
200	  % \(\mathscr K\) generated by \(f_i^{-1}\) on \(U_i\). This is well-defined,
201	  % since \(f_i/f_j\) is invertible on \(U_i \cap U_j\), and \(f_i^{-1}\) and
202	  % \(f_j^{-1}\) generate the same \(\mathscr O_X\)-module. We call
203	  % \(\mathscr L(D)\) the \emph{sheaf associated to \(D\)}."
204	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Proposition 6.13. \emph{Let \(X\) be a scheme. Then:
205	  % (a) for any Cartier divisor \(D\), \(\mathscr L(D)\) is an invertible
206	  % sheaf on \(X\).}"
207	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p.~144 (definition of
208	  \(\mathscr L(D)\) and Proposition~6.13(a)).}
209	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over \(\bar k\) and let \(P \in C\) be a
210	  closed point. Let \(\mathfrak m_P \subset \mathcal O_{C, P}\) denote the
211	  maximal ideal of the local ring at \(P\); by smoothness of \(C\),
212	  \(\mathcal O_{C, P}\) is a discrete valuation ring and \(\mathfrak m_P\)
213	  is generated by any uniformizer \(f_P \in \mathcal O_{C, P}\) (an
214	  element of \(\mathfrak m_P \setminus \mathfrak m_P^2\)). The Cartier
215	  divisor associated to the closed point \(P\) is the effective
216	  Cartier divisor cut out locally by the uniformizer \(f_P\) on an open
217	  affine neighbourhood of \(P\), and by the unit \(1\) on the complement
218	  \(C \setminus \{P\}\). The \emph{line bundle of the closed point \(P\)},
219	  denoted \(\mathcal O_C(P)\), is the locally free \(\mathcal O_C\)-module
220	  of rank \(1\) defined by Hartshorne's construction \(\mathscr L(D)\)
221	  applied to this Cartier divisor: explicitly, \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) is the
222	  sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-module of the sheaf of total quotient rings
223	  \(\mathcal K_C\) (the constant sheaf of the function field \(K(C)\) on the
224	  integral scheme \(C\)) that is generated by \(f_P^{-1} \in K(C)\) on a
225	  neighbourhood of \(P\) and by \(1 \in K(C)\) on the complement
226	  \(C \setminus \{P\}\). Hartshorne's Proposition~6.13(a) confirms that
227	  this subsheaf is an invertible sheaf, independently of the choice of
228	  uniformizer \(f_P\) (any two uniformizers differ by a unit in
229	  \(\mathcal O_{C, P}^{\times}\), so they generate the same
230	  \(\mathcal O_C\)-submodule of \(\mathcal K_C\)).
231	
232	  \paragraph{Equivalent description via the ideal sheaf.} Let
233	  \(\mathcal I_P \subset \mathcal O_C\) denote the ideal sheaf of the
234	  closed point \(P\) (locally generated by the uniformizer \(f_P\) near
235	  \(P\), and equal to \(\mathcal O_C\) on the complement). Then
236	  \(\mathcal I_P\) is the line bundle \(\mathcal O_C(-P)\) associated to the
237	  Weil divisor \(-[P]\), and \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) is the
238	  \(\mathcal O_C\)-linear dual:
239	  \[
240	    \mathcal O_C(P) \;\cong\; \mathcal H\!om_{\mathcal O_C}(\mathcal I_P,
241	    \mathcal O_C)
242	    \;\cong\; \mathcal I_P^{-1}.
243	  \]
244	  Both descriptions yield the same invertible sheaf; the
245	  ``subsheaf of \(\mathcal K_C\)'' description above is the one used in
246	  the prose, while the ``dual of the ideal sheaf'' description is the
247	  most direct entry point for the Lean construction (Mathlib's
248	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData} packages
249	  quasi-coherent ideal sheaves, from which the dual is constructed via
250	  the internal-hom on invertible sheaves).
251	
252	  \paragraph{Lean signature scope.} The Lean definition
253	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint} takes a
254	  scheme \(C\), a point \(P : C\), and a closedness witness
255	  \(\texttt{IsClosed (\{P\} : Set C)}\) (the same input shape as
256	  REF), and returns an invertible
257	  \(\mathcal O_C\)-module \(\mathcal O_C(P)\). The well-definedness as an
258	  invertible sheaf is part of the construction and goes through
259	  smoothness of \(C\) (delivering the DVR stalk at \(P\)) and the standard
260	  ideal-sheaf-of-a-closed-point machinery in Mathlib's
261	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IdealSheaf} chapter. The chapter's
262	  consumption of the closed-point hypothesis matches \texttt{RR.1}:
263	  closed plus dimension-\(1\)-integral delivers \(\mathrm{codim}_C \{P\} = 1\)
264	  automatically. The declaration is \texttt{noncomputable}.
265	\end{definition}

267	\begin{definition}
268	
269	\leanok
270	[Inclusion of \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) into the function field]
271	  \label{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_toFunctionField}
272	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField}
273	  
274	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, p.~144 (definition of \(\mathscr L(D)\) as a
275	  % subsheaf of the sheaf of total quotient rings \(\mathscr K\); the
276	  % canonical inclusion \(\mathscr L(D) \hookrightarrow \mathscr K\) is part
277	  % of the construction) (read from
278	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 161).
279	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Definition. Let \(D\) be a Cartier divisor on a scheme
280	  % \(X\), represented by \(\{(U_i, f_i)\}\) as above. We define a subsheaf
281	  % \(\mathscr L(D)\) of the sheaf of total quotient rings \(\mathscr K\) by
282	  % taking \(\mathscr L(D)\) to be the sub-\(\mathscr O_X\)-module of
283	  % \(\mathscr K\) generated by \(f_i^{-1}\) on \(U_i\). This is well-defined,
284	  % since \(f_i/f_j\) is invertible on \(U_i \cap U_j\), and \(f_i^{-1}\) and
285	  % \(f_j^{-1}\) generate the same \(\mathscr O_X\)-module. We call
286	  % \(\mathscr L(D)\) the \emph{sheaf associated to \(D\)}."
287	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p.~144 (definition of \(\mathscr L(D)\)
288	  as a subsheaf of \(\mathscr K\)).}
289	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\)
290	  with \(C\) integral, and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. The line bundle
291	  \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) of REF is by
292	  construction a sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-module of the sheaf of total quotient
293	  rings \(\mathcal K_C\). Since \(C\) is integral, \(\mathcal K_C\) is the
294	  constant sheaf at the function field \(K(C)\), and taking global sections
295	  of the structural inclusion \(\mathcal O_C(P) \hookrightarrow \mathcal K_C\)
296	  produces a canonical \(\bar k\)-linear map
297	  \[
298	    \iota \;\colon\; H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))
299	      \;\longrightarrow\; H^0(C, \mathcal K_C) \;\cong\; K(C)
300	  \]
301	  sending a global section \(s \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) to the rational
302	  function on \(C\) obtained by composing \(s\) with the inclusion of
303	  \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) into \(\mathcal K_C\) at each point. The map \(\iota\) is
304	  injective (the sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-module $\mathcal O_C(P) \subset
305	  \mathcal K_C\( is torsion-free of rank \)1$, so taking global sections
306	  preserves injectivity on the integral scheme \(C\)).
307	
308	  \paragraph{Lean signature scope.} The Lean definition

311	  \(C\), a point \(P\), and the closedness witness
312	  \texttt{IsClosed (\{P\} : Set C.left)}) and produces, for each global

321	\section{Global sections as a Riemann--Roch space}
322	
323	\begin{lemma}
324	
325	
326	\leanok
327	[Global sections of \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) as rational functions with a
328	controlled pole]
329	  \label{lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff}
330	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff}
331	  
332	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.7, Proposition~7.7, p.~157 (effective
333	  % divisors linearly equivalent to \(D_0\) are the divisors of zeros of
334	  % global sections of \(\mathscr L(D_0)\), with the explicit
335	  % identification of those global sections with rational functions
336	  % \(f\) satisfying \((f) \geq -D_0\)) (read from
337	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 174).
338	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Proposition 7.7. \emph{Let \(X\) be a nonsingular
339	  % projective variety over the algebraically closed field \(k\). Let
340	  % \(D_0\) be a divisor on \(X\) and let \(\mathscr L = \mathscr L(D_0)\)
341	  % be the corresponding invertible sheaf. Then:
342	  % (a) for each nonzero \(s \in \Gamma(X, \mathscr L)\), the divisor of
343	  % zeros \((s)_0\) is an effective divisor linearly equivalent to \(D_0\);
344	  % (b) every effective divisor linearly equivalent to \(D_0\) is \((s)_0\)
345	  % for some \(s \in \Gamma(X, \mathscr L)\); and
346	  % (c) two sections \(s, s' \in \Gamma(X, \mathscr L)\) have the same
347	  % divisor of zeros if and only if there is a \(\lambda \in k^*\) such
348	  % that \(s' = \lambda s\).}"
349	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(b) If \(D \geq 0\) and \(D = D_0 + (f)\), then
350	  % \((f) \geq -D_0\). Thus \(f\) gives a global section of
351	  % \(\mathscr L(D_0)\) whose divisor of zeros is \(D\)."
352	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.7, Proposition~7.7 and its
353	  proof of part (b), p.~157.}
354	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over \(\bar k\), let \(P \in C\) be a
355	  closed point, and let \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) be the line bundle of
356	  REF. The \(\bar k\)-vector space of
357	  global sections of \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) is naturally identified with the
358	  Riemann--Roch space
359	  \[
360	    L([P]) \;:=\;
361	    \bigl\{f \in K(C)^{\times} \;\big|\; \div(f) + [P] \geq 0
362	    \text{ in } \mathrm{Div}(C)\bigr\}
363	    \,\cup\, \{0\}.
364	  \]
365	  Equivalently, the order conditions
366	  \[
367	    \ord_Q(f) \;\geq\; 0 \quad \text{for every closed point } Q \in C
368	    \text{ with } Q \neq P,
369	    \qquad
370	    \ord_P(f) \;\geq\; -1
371	  \]
372	  on \(f \in K(C)^{\times}\) are equivalent to the existence of a global
373	  section \(s \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) whose image \(\iota(s)\) under
374	  the canonical inclusion $\iota : \mathcal O_C(P) \hookrightarrow
375	  \mathcal K_C \cong K(C)\( equals \)f\( (the iff binds \)s\( to \)f$
376	  explicitly — vacuous-in-\(f\) readings such as ``some nonzero global
377	  section exists'' are excluded by construction).
378	
379	  Under this identification, the inclusion
380	  \(\bar k \hookrightarrow H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) of constant functions
381	  corresponds to the natural map $\mathcal O_C \hookrightarrow
382	  \mathcal O_C(P)\( exhibiting \)\mathcal O_C(P)$ as the twist of the
383	  structure sheaf along the prime divisor \([P]\).
384	\end{lemma}
385	
386	\begin{proof}
387	  
388	  
389	  By construction, \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) is the sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-module of
390	  the sheaf of total quotient rings \(\mathcal K_C\) generated locally by
391	  \(f_P^{-1}\) near \(P\) and by \(1\) on \(C \setminus \{P\}\), where \(f_P\) is
392	  a uniformizer at \(P\) (REF). Since \(C\)
393	  is integral, \(\mathcal K_C\) is the constant sheaf at the function field
394	  \(K(C)\), and a global section of any sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-module
395	  \(\mathcal F \subset \mathcal K_C\) is precisely an element of \(K(C)\)
396	  whose germ at every point lies in \(\mathcal F\) at that point.
397	  Therefore \(f \in K(C)\) is a global section of \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) if
398	  and only if:
399	
400	  \begin{itemize}
401	    \item For every closed point \(Q \in C\) with \(Q \neq P\), the germ of
402	      \(f\) at \(Q\) lies in \(\mathcal O_C(P)_Q = \mathcal O_{C, Q}\)
403	      (the structure sheaf, since \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) agrees with
404	      \(\mathcal O_C\) on \(C \setminus \{P\}\)); equivalently,
405	      \(\ord_Q(f) \geq 0\).
406	    \item At the closed point \(P\), the germ of \(f\) at \(P\) lies in
407	      \(\mathcal O_C(P)_P = f_P^{-1} \cdot \mathcal O_{C, P}\);
408	      equivalently, \(f_P \cdot f \in \mathcal O_{C, P}\), i.e.\
409	      \(\ord_P(f_P \cdot f) \geq 0\), i.e.\ \(\ord_P(f) \geq -1\).
410	  \end{itemize}
411	  In divisorial language (REF,
412	  REF), the two conditions combine to
413	  \(\div(f) + [P] \geq 0\), recovering the Riemann--Roch space description
414	  \(L([P])\). The zero element of \(K(C)\) is included by convention as the
415	  zero global section.
416	
417	  The identification with Hartshorne's Proposition~7.7(b) is direct:
418	  taking \(D_0 = [P]\) effective, Hartshorne's argument shows that a
419	  rational function \(f\) with \((f) \geq -D_0\) defines a global section
420	  of \(\mathscr L(D_0)\). The condition \((f) \geq -D_0 = -[P]\) is exactly
421	  \(\div(f) + [P] \geq 0\). The compatibility with the constant inclusion
422	  follows by taking \(f = 1 \in K(C)^{\times}\): then \(\div(1) = 0\), so
423	  \(\div(1) + [P] = [P] \geq 0\), and the corresponding section of
424	  \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) is the image of $1 \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C)
425	  \cong \bar k\( under the structural inclusion \)\mathcal O_C
426	  \hookrightarrow \mathcal O_C(P)$.
427	\end{proof}

429	\section{Cohomological vanishing in genus zero}
430	
431	\begin{lemma}
432	
433	
434	\leanok
435	[Vanishing of \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) on a genus-\(0\) curve]
436	  \label{lem:H1_vanishing_lineBundleAtClosedPoint_genusZero}
437	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.h1_vanishing_genusZero}
438	  
439	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, the proof of Theorem~1.3 (the inductive
440	  % step ``Next, let \(D\) be any divisor, and let \(P\) be any point.''):
441	  % the short exact sequence $0 \to \mathscr L(-P) \to \mathscr O_X \to
442	  % k(P) \to 0\( and its tensor with \)\mathscr L(D + P)$ giving
443	  % \(0 \to \mathscr L(D) \to \mathscr L(D + P) \to k(P) \to 0\)
444	  % (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 313).
445	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We consider \(P\) as a closed subscheme of \(X\). Its
446	  % structure sheaf is a skyscraper sheaf \(k\) sitting at the point \(P\),
447	  % which we denote by \(k(P)\), and its ideal sheaf is \(\mathscr L(-P)\)
448	  % by (II, 6.18). Therefore we have an exact sequence
449	  % \(0 \to \mathscr L(-P) \to \mathscr O_X \to k(P) \to 0\).
450	  % Tensoring with \(\mathscr L(D + P)\) we get
451	  % \(0 \to \mathscr L(D) \to \mathscr L(D + P) \to k(P) \to 0\),
452	  % (since \(\mathscr L(P)\) is locally free of rank 1, tensoring by it
453	  % does not affect the sheaf \(k(P)\))."
454	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, p.~296, the inductive step of the
455	  proof of Theorem~1.3.}
456	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
457	  \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. Then
458	  \[
459	    H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \;=\; 0,
460	  \]
461	  i.e.\ \(\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 0\).
462	\end{lemma}
463	
464	\begin{proof}
465	  
466	  Specialise Hartshorne's inductive step at \(D = 0\). The standard short
467	  exact sequence
468	  \[
469	    0 \to \mathcal O_C(-[P]) \to \mathcal O_C \to k(P) \to 0
470	  \]
471	  (Hartshorne~II.6.18: the ideal sheaf of the locally principal closed
472	  subscheme \(P\) is \(\mathcal O_C(-[P])\); the quotient is the structure
473	  sheaf of \(P\), which is the skyscraper sheaf \(k(P) \cong \bar k\)
474	  supported at \(P\) since \(P\) has residue field \(\bar k\) by closedness
475	  over the algebraically closed base \(\bar k\)). Tensoring with the
476	  locally free rank-\(1\) sheaf \(\mathcal O_C([P])\)
477	  (REF) preserves exactness and leaves
478	  the skyscraper sheaf \(k(P)\) unchanged, giving
479	  \[
480	    0 \to \mathcal O_C \to \mathcal O_C(P) \to k(P) \to 0
481	  \]
482	  in \(\mathbf{Coh}(C)\). Apply the long exact sequence of sheaf
483	  cohomology (REF, the project's $\Module \bar
484	  k\(-flavoured cohomology pipeline). Using that \)\dim C = 1$ kills
485	  \(H^i\) for \(i \geq 2\) (Grothendieck vanishing on a \(1\)-dimensional
486	  scheme), the long exact sequence reads
487	  \begin{multline*}
488	    0 \to H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) \to H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \to H^0(C, k(P)) \\
489	    \to H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) \to H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \to H^1(C, k(P)) \to 0.
490	  \end{multline*}
491	  Two ingredients close the proof:
492	
493	  \emph{(a) \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0\).} This is the genus-\(0\)
494	  hypothesis: by REF,
495	  \[
496	    g(C) \;=\; \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C),
497	  \]
498	  so \(g(C) = 0\) is exactly \(\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0\),
499	  i.e.\ \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0\) as a \(\bar k\)-vector space.
500	
501	  \emph{(b) \(H^1(C, k(P)) = 0\).} The skyscraper sheaf \(k(P)\) is
502	  supported at a single closed point, hence is flasque: every section
503	  on an open \(U \subset C\) either restricts to \(0\) (if \(P \notin U\)) or
504	  takes values in the stalk \(k(P)_P \cong \bar k\) (if \(P \in U\)), and
505	  restriction maps between opens of either type are surjective. A
506	  flasque sheaf on any topological space has vanishing higher
507	  cohomology (Hartshorne~III.2.5); in particular
508	  \(H^i(C, k(P)) = 0\) for \(i \geq 1\), and \emph{a fortiori}
509	  \(H^1(C, k(P)) = 0\).
510	
511	  Substituting (a) and (b) into the long exact sequence collapses the
512	  segment ending at \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) to
513	  \[
514	    0 \to H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \to 0,
515	  \]
516	  whence \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 0\), as required.
517	
518	  \emph{Lean reference note on the long exact sequence.} Mathlib
519	  (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) ships the long exact sequence of
520	  \(\Ext_{\mathcal O_C}^*(\mathbf 1, -)\) at the abelian-group level
521	  (\texttt{CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariantSequence\_exact}); the
522	  project's \(\Module \bar k\)-flavoured cohomology pipeline
523	  (REF) inherits the long exact sequence by
524	  forget-functor naturality. The skyscraper-cohomology vanishing
525	  \(H^i(C, k(P)) = 0\) for \(i \geq 1\) is closed by either the flasque
526	  argument (Hartshorne~III.2.5; Mathlib has the abelian-sheaf form
527	  via \texttt{CategoryTheory.Sheaf.Flasque}) or, more directly for the
528	  curve case, by the affine-cover Čech-cohomology argument on
529	  \(C \setminus \{P\}\) and a small affine open around \(P\) (the
530	  skyscraper has zero sections on \(C \setminus \{P\}\), so the Čech
531	  complex degenerates).
532	\end{proof}
533	
534	\section{The dimension formula in genus zero}
535	
536	\begin{theorem}
537	
538	
539	\leanok
540	[\(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2\) on a genus-\(0\) curve]
541	  \label{thm:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_dim_eq_two_of_genusZero}
542	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.dim_eq_two_of_genusZero}
543	  
544	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, the Riemann--Roch chain inside
545	  % Example~1.3.5 applied with \(D = [P]\): $\chi(\mathscr L([P]))
546	  % = \deg([P]) + 1 - g = 1 + 1 - 0 = 2$, combined with the vanishing
547	  % \(H^1(\mathscr L([P])) = 0\) (read from
548	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages 312, 314).
549	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Theorem 1.3 (Riemann--Roch). \emph{Let \(D\) be a
550	  % divisor on a curve \(X\) of genus \(g\). Then}
551	  % \(l(D) - l(K - D) = \deg D + 1 - g\)."
552	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we have to show that for any \(D\),
553	  % \(\chi(\mathscr L(D)) = \deg D + 1 - g\),
554	  % where for any coherent sheaf \(\mathscr F\) on \(X\), \(\chi(\mathscr F)\)
555	  % is the Euler characteristic
556	  % \(\chi(\mathscr F) = \dim H^0(X, \mathscr F) - \dim H^1(X, \mathscr F)\)."
557	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Theorem~1.3, p.~295 (the
558	  \(\chi\)-identity inside the proof) and Example~1.3.5, p.~297 (the
559	  genus-\(0\) specialisation chain).}
560	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
561	  \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. Then
562	  \[
563	    \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \;=\; 2.
564	  \]
565	\end{theorem}
566	
567	\begin{proof}
568	  
569	  Specialise the Euler-characteristic identity
570	  REF of \texttt{RR.2} to
571	  the divisor \(D = [P]\) of REF. The
572	  degree of \([P]\) is \(\deg([P]) = 1\) (every closed point contributes
573	  \(1\) to the bare-sum degree of REF), and the
574	  genus hypothesis \(g(C) = 0\) (REF) gives
575	  \[
576	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(P)) \;=\; \deg([P]) + 1 - g(C)
577	    \;=\; 1 + 1 - 0 \;=\; 2.
578	  \]
579	  Unfolding \(\chi\) via REF as a two-term Euler
580	  characteristic on a curve, this rewrites as
581	  \[
582	    \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))
583	    \,-\, \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P))
584	    \;=\; 2.
585	  \]
586	  By
587	  REF, the second
588	  term vanishes:
589	  \(\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 0\). Substituting yields
590	  \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2\), as required.
591	
592	  \emph{Alternative direct route via the long exact sequence.} The
593	  same long exact sequence used in the proof of
594	  REF computes
595	  the dimension of \(H^0\) in one stroke without invoking
596	  \texttt{RR.2}: from
597	  \[
598	    0 \to H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) \to H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))
599	    \to H^0(C, k(P)) \to H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) \to \cdots
600	  \]
601	  together with \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) = 1\)
602	  (Hartshorne~I.3.4: \(H^0\) of the structure sheaf on a proper
603	  geometrically irreducible variety over \(\bar k\) is \(\bar k\)),
604	  \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, k(P)) = 1\) (skyscraper at a \(\bar k\)-rational
605	  point), and \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0\) (genus-\(0\)), the connecting
606	  map \(H^0(C, k(P)) \to H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)\) has zero codomain and the
607	  segment yields the short exact sequence
608	  \[
609	    0 \to \bar k \to H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \to \bar k \to 0
610	  \]
611	  of \(\bar k\)-vector spaces, whence $\dim_{\bar k}
612	  H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 1 + 1 = 2$. This alternative route does
613	  not consume the Euler-characteristic identity of \texttt{RR.2} and
614	  could be substituted at the formalisation stage; the chapter uses
615	  \texttt{RR.2} as written above to keep the dependency graph in
616	  line with the iter-173 RR sub-build sequencing.
617	\end{proof}

619	\section{Existence of a non-constant rational function}
620	
621	\begin{corollary}
622	
623	
624	\leanok
625	[A genus-\(0\) curve admits a non-constant rational function with a
626	single simple pole]
627	  \label{cor:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_exists_nonconstant_genusZero}
628	  % Alias label consumed under the shorter name by
629	  % \texttt{RiemannRoch\_RationalCurveIso.tex} (RR.4):
630	  \label{cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero}
631	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.exists_nonconstant_genusZero}
632	  
633	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, Exercise~1.1 (the existence of a
634	  % nonconstant rational function regular everywhere except at one
635	  % point on a genus-\(0\) curve, as a direct consequence of the
636	  % Riemann--Roch dimension formula) (read from
637	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314).
638	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "1.1. Let \(X\) be a curve, and let \(P \in X\) be a
639	  % point. Then there exists a nonconstant rational function $f \in
640	  % K(X)\(, which is regular everywhere except at \)P$."
641	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Exercise~1.1, p.~297.}
642	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
643	  \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. Then
644	  there exists a non-constant rational function \(f \in K(C)\) such that:
645	  \begin{itemize}
646	    \item \(f\) is regular on \(C \setminus \{P\}\), i.e.\ \(\ord_Q(f) \geq 0\)
647	      for every closed point \(Q \in C\) with \(Q \neq P\);
648	    \item \(f\) has at most a simple pole at \(P\), i.e.\
649	      \(\ord_P(f) \geq -1\);
650	    \item \(f \notin \bar k\), equivalently
651	      \(\div(f) \neq 0 \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\).
652	  \end{itemize}
653	  In particular, the pair \((1, f)\) spans
654	  \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) as a \(\bar k\)-vector space.
655	\end{corollary}
656	
657	\begin{proof}
658	  
659	  By
660	  REF,
661	  \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2\). The constant subspace
662	  \(\bar k \cdot 1 \subset H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\), obtained from the
663	  structural inclusion $\bar k \cong H^0(C, \mathcal O_C)
664	  \hookrightarrow H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))$ (the rational function
665	  \(f = 1\), with \(\div(1) + [P] = [P] \geq 0\), under the
666	  identification of
667	  REF), is a one-dimensional
668	  \(\bar k\)-vector subspace. Since the ambient space has dimension \(2\),
669	  the quotient \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) / \bar k \cdot 1\) is a
670	  non-zero one-dimensional \(\bar k\)-vector space; any lift to
671	  \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) of a non-zero element of the quotient is
672	  an element of \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \setminus \bar k \cdot 1\).
673	
674	  Choose such a lift $s \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \setminus \bar k
675	  \cdot 1$. Under the identification of
676	  REF the section
677	  \(s\) corresponds to a rational function \(f \in K(C)^{\times}\)
678	  satisfying \(\div(f) + [P] \geq 0\), equivalently \(\ord_Q(f) \geq 0\)
679	  for every \(Q \neq P\) and \(\ord_P(f) \geq -1\). The choice
680	  \(s \notin \bar k \cdot 1\) means \(f\) is not the image of a constant,
681	  so \(f \notin \bar k\) (every non-zero element of
682	  \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) \cong \bar k\) corresponds, under the
683	  identification, to a constant element of \(K(C)\)). The three bullets
684	  of the statement are now immediate.
685	
686	  Spanning. Linear independence of \(1\) and \(f\) over \(\bar k\) in
687	  \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) is the same condition as
688	  \(s \notin \bar k \cdot 1\) above; combined with the dimension count
689	  \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2\), the pair \((1, f)\) is a
690	  \(\bar k\)-basis of \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\).
691	
692	  \paragraph{Lean signature scope.} The Lean signature
693	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.exists\_nonconstant\_genusZero}
694	  returns an element \(f\) of the project's function-field carrier
695	  \(K(C)\) together with proofs of regularity on \(C \setminus \{P\}\) and
696	  the simple-pole bound at \(P\), packaged in the same shape as
697	  REF's output. The
698	  non-constancy is recorded as a separate field; the spanning
699	  statement is downstream and is consumed by \texttt{RR.4} via
700	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections}.
701	\end{proof}

970	For a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×`, the following are
971	equivalent:
972	
973	* there exists a global section `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under
974	  the canonical inclusion `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` equals `f` (formally,

977	  `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose generic point is not `P`, and
978	  `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (where the latter is read off the prime divisor
979	  `⟨P, h⟩` with `h : Order.coheight P = 1` the codimension-one witness
980	  automatic for a closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme).

982	The iff is the substantive content of Hartshorne's Proposition 7.7(b) /
983	its proof, specialised to `D_0 = [P]`.

## Lane A — `RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean` (RR.3) — Subfunctor refactor → Case B close

**Target**: with iter-189 plan-phase refactor agent
`ocofp-subfunctor-restructure` landing `carrierTypeSubfunctor` +
restructured `carrierPresheaf_isSheaf` body via
`CategoryTheory.Subfunctor.isSheaf_iff`, the prover closes the
remaining Case B residual.

**Recipe** (post-refactor):
1. The refactor agent restructures `carrierPresheaf_isSheaf` body
   to call `carrierTypeSubfunctor.isSheaf_iff.mpr` (or equivalent
   Mathlib idiom) with the stalk-locality predicate.
2. The stalk-locality predicate reduces to: for any cover `{U_i}` of
   `U`, any matching family `{s_i : carrier(U_i)}` glues to a unique
   `s : carrier(U)` — proved via irreducibility (any two non-empty
   opens of an irreducible scheme intersect, so the matching values
   coincide on overlaps).
3. The new structure handles `⊥` cleanly: `carrierTypeSubfunctor`
   imposes `(carrier-condition) ∧ (U ≠ ⊥ ∨ f = 0)` at the SET level,
   so the empty-cover case is absorbed in the trivial-at-⊥ part.

**Substrate**: Mathlib at b80f227 has `CategoryTheory.Subfunctor`
(verify location via `lean_local_search Subfunctor`); the
sheaf-condition equivalence is via Subfunctor / sub-presheaf framework.

**HARD BAR**: ≥1 sorry close iter-189. Helper budget = 1.

**Effort**: ~30-60 LOC axiom-clean post-refactor.

added `def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierSubmoduleSheaf` block
pinning iter-188's `⊓ trivAtBot` carrier-refinement wrapper.

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Elementwise
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FunctorCategory.EpiMono
10	public import Mathlib.Tactic.CategoryTheory.Elementwise

12	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Subfunctor.Image
13	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Subfunctor.Sieves
14	
15	/-!
16	
17	# Subsheaf of types
18	
19	We define the subsheaf of a type-valued presheaf.
20	
21	## Main results
22	
23	- `CategoryTheory.Subfunctor.sheafify` :
24	  The sheafification of a subpresheaf as a subpresheaf. Note that this is a sheaf only when the
25	  whole sheaf is.
26	- `CategoryTheory.Subfunctor.sheafify_isSheaf` :
27	  The sheafification is a sheaf
28	- `CategoryTheory.Subfunctor.sheafifyLift` :
29	  The descent of a map into a sheaf to the sheafification.
30	- `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.imageSheaf` : The image sheaf of a morphism.
31	- `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.imageFactorization` : The image sheaf as a
32	  `Limits.imageFactorization`.
33	-/
34	
35	@[expose] public section
36	
37	
38	universe w v u
39	
40	open Opposite CategoryTheory
41	
42	namespace CategoryTheory
43	
44	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
45	
46	variable {F F' F'' : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type w} (G G' : Subfunctor F)
47	
48	/-- Every subpresheaf of a separated presheaf is itself separated. -/
49	theorem Subfunctor.isSeparated {J : GrothendieckTopology C} (h : Presieve.IsSeparated J F) :
50	    Presieve.IsSeparated J G.toFunctor :=
51	  fun _ S hS _ _ _ hx₁ hx₂ ↦ Subtype.ext <| h S hS _ _ _ (hx₁.map G.ι) (hx₂.map G.ι)
52	
53	@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-11")] alias Subpresheaf.isSeparated := Subfunctor.isSeparated
54	
55	/-- The sheafification of a subpresheaf as a subpresheaf.
56	Note that this is a sheaf only when the whole presheaf is a sheaf. -/
57	def Subfunctor.sheafify : Subfunctor F where
58	  obj U := { s | G.sieveOfSection s ∈ J (unop U) }
59	  map := by
60	    rintro U V i s hs
61	    refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.pullback_stable i.unop hs)
62	    intro _ _ h
63	    dsimp at h ⊢
64	    rwa [← comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp]
65	
66	@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-11")] alias Subpresheaf.sheafify := Subfunctor.sheafify
67	
68	theorem Subfunctor.le_sheafify : G ≤ G.sheafify J := by
69	  intro U s hs
70	  change _ ∈ J _
71	  convert J.top_mem U.unop
72	  rw [eq_top_iff]
73	  rintro V i -
74	  exact G.map i.op hs
75	
76	@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-11")] alias Subpresheaf.le_sheafify := Subfunctor.le_sheafify
77	
78	variable {J}
79	
80	theorem Subfunctor.eq_sheafify (h : Presieve.IsSheaf J F) (hG : Presieve.IsSheaf J G.toFunctor) :
81	    G = G.sheafify J := by
82	  apply (G.le_sheafify J).antisymm
83	  intro U s hs
84	  suffices ((hG _ hs).amalgamate _ (G.family_of_elements_compatible s)).1 = s by
85	    rw [← this]
86	    exact ((hG _ hs).amalgamate _ (G.family_of_elements_compatible s)).2
87	  apply (h _ hs).isSeparatedFor.ext
88	  intro V i hi
89	  exact (congr_arg Subtype.val ((hG _ hs).valid_glue (G.family_of_elements_compatible s) _ hi) :)
90	
91	@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-11")] alias Subpresheaf.eq_sheafify := Subfunctor.eq_sheafify
92	
93	theorem Subfunctor.sheafify_isSheaf (hF : Presieve.IsSheaf J F) :
94	    Presieve.IsSheaf J (G.sheafify J).toFunctor := by
95	  refine (isSeparated _ hF.isSeparated).isSheaf fun U S hS x hx ↦ ?_
96	  let S' := Sieve.bind S fun Y f hf => G.sieveOfSection (x f hf).1
97	  have := fun (V) (i : V ⟶ U) (hi : S' i) => hi
98	  choose W i₁ i₂ hi₂ h₁ h₂ using this
99	  dsimp [-Sieve.bind_apply] at *
100	  let x'' : Presieve.FamilyOfElements F S' := fun V i hi => F.map (i₁ V i hi).op (x _ (hi₂ V i hi))
101	  have H : ∀ s, x''.IsAmalgamation s.1 → x.IsAmalgamation s := by
102	    intro s H V i hi
103	    refine Subtype.ext ?_
104	    apply (hF _ (x i hi).2).isSeparatedFor.ext
105	    intro V' i' hi'
106	    have hi'' : S' (i' ≫ i) := ⟨_, _, _, hi, hi', rfl⟩
107	    have := H _ hi''
108	    rw [op_comp, F.map_comp] at this
109	    exact this.trans (congr_arg Subtype.val (hx _ _ (hi₂ _ _ hi'') hi (h₂ _ _ hi'')))
110	  have : x''.Compatible := by
111	    intro V₁ V₂ V₃ g₁ g₂ g₃ g₄ S₁ S₂ e
112	    rw [← comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← comp_apply, Functor.map_comp]
113	    simpa using
114	      congr_arg Subtype.val
115	        (hx (g₁ ≫ i₁ _ _ S₁) (g₂ ≫ i₁ _ _ S₂) (hi₂ _ _ S₁) (hi₂ _ _ S₂)
116	        (by simp only [Category.assoc, h₂, e]))
117	  obtain ⟨t, ht, ht'⟩ := hF _ (J.bind_covering hS fun V i hi => (x i hi).2) _ this
118	  refine ⟨⟨t, _⟩, H ⟨t, ?_⟩ ht⟩
119	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.bind_covering hS fun V i hi => (x i hi).2)
120	  intro V i hi
121	  dsimp
122	  rw [ht _ hi]
123	  exact h₁ _ _ hi
124	
125	@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-11")]
126	alias Subpresheaf.sheafify_isSheaf := Subfunctor.sheafify_isSheaf
127	
128	theorem Subfunctor.eq_sheafify_iff (h : Presieve.IsSheaf J F) :
129	    G = G.sheafify J ↔ Presieve.IsSheaf J G.toFunctor :=
130	  ⟨fun e => e.symm ▸ G.sheafify_isSheaf h, G.eq_sheafify h⟩
131	
132	@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-11")]
133	alias Subpresheaf.eq_sheafify_iff := Subfunctor.eq_sheafify_iff
134	
135	theorem Subfunctor.isSheaf_iff (h : Presieve.IsSheaf J F) :
136	    Presieve.IsSheaf J G.toFunctor ↔
137	      ∀ (U) (s : F.obj U), G.sieveOfSection s ∈ J (unop U) → s ∈ G.obj U := by
138	  rw [← G.eq_sheafify_iff h]
139	  change _ ↔ G.sheafify J ≤ G
140	  exact ⟨Eq.ge, (G.le_sheafify J).antisymm⟩
141	
142	@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-11")] alias Subpresheaf.isSheaf_iff := Subfunctor.isSheaf_iff
143	
144	theorem Subfunctor.sheafify_sheafify (h : Presieve.IsSheaf J F) :
145	    (G.sheafify J).sheafify J = G.sheafify J :=
146	  ((Subfunctor.eq_sheafify_iff _ h).mpr <| G.sheafify_isSheaf h).symm

51	namespace Presheaf
52	
53	section
54	
55	variable {X : TopCat.{x}} (F : Presheaf C X) {ι : Type*} (U : ι → Opens X)
56	
57	/-- A family of sections `sf` is compatible, if the restrictions of `sf i` and `sf j` to `U i ⊓ U j`
58	agree, for all `i` and `j`
59	-/
60	def IsCompatible (sf : ∀ i : ι, ToType (F.obj (op (U i)))) : Prop :=
61	  ∀ i j : ι, F.map (infLELeft (U i) (U j)).op (sf i) = F.map (infLERight (U i) (U j)).op (sf j)
62	
63	/-- A section `s` is a gluing for a family of sections `sf` if it restricts to `sf i` on `U i`,
64	for all `i`
65	-/
66	def IsGluing (sf : ∀ i : ι, ToType (F.obj (op (U i)))) (s : ToType (F.obj (op (iSup U)))) : Prop :=
67	  ∀ i : ι, F.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op s = sf i
68	
69	/--
70	The sheaf condition in terms of unique gluings. A presheaf `F : Presheaf C X` satisfies this sheaf
71	condition if and only if, for every compatible family of sections `sf : Π i : ι, F.obj (op (U i))`,
72	there exists a unique gluing `s : F.obj (op (iSup U))`.
73	
74	We prove this to be equivalent to the usual one below in
75	`TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheafUniqueGluing`
76	-/
77	def IsSheafUniqueGluing : Prop :=
78	  ∀ ⦃ι : Type x⦄ (U : ι → Opens X) (sf : ∀ i : ι, ToType (F.obj (op (U i)))),
79	    IsCompatible F U sf → ∃! s : ToType (F.obj (op (iSup U))), IsGluing F U sf s
80	
81	end
82	
83	section TypeValued
84	
85	variable {X : TopCat.{x}} {F : Presheaf Type* X} {ι : Type*} {U : ι → Opens X}
86	
87	/-- Given sections over a family of open sets, extend it to include
88	  sections over pairwise intersections of the open sets. -/
89	def objPairwiseOfFamily (sf : ∀ i, F.obj (op (U i))) :
90	    ∀ i, ((Pairwise.diagram U).op ⋙ F).obj i
91	  | ⟨Pairwise.single i⟩ => sf i
92	  | ⟨Pairwise.pair i j⟩ => F.map (infLELeft (U i) (U j)).op (sf i)
93	
94	/-- Given a compatible family of sections over open sets, extend it to a
95	  section of the functor `(Pairwise.diagram U).op ⋙ F`. -/
96	def IsCompatible.sectionPairwise {sf} (h : IsCompatible F U sf) :
97	    ((Pairwise.diagram U).op ⋙ F).sections := by
98	  refine ⟨objPairwiseOfFamily sf, ?_⟩
99	  let G := (Pairwise.diagram U).op ⋙ F
100	  rintro (i | ⟨i, j⟩) (i' | ⟨i', j'⟩) (_ | _ | _ | _)
101	  · exact ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (G.map_id <| op <| Pairwise.single i) _
102	  · rfl
103	  · exact (h i' i).symm
104	  · exact ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (G.map_id <| op <| Pairwise.pair i j) _
105	
106	theorem isGluing_iff_pairwise {sf s} : IsGluing F U sf s ↔
107	    ∀ i, (F.mapCone (Pairwise.cocone U).op).π.app i s = objPairwiseOfFamily sf i := by
108	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun h i ↦ h (op <| Pairwise.single i)⟩
109	  rintro (i | ⟨i, j⟩)
110	  · exact h i
111	  · rw [← (F.mapCone (Pairwise.cocone U).op).w (op <| Pairwise.Hom.left i j)]
112	    exact congr_arg _ (h i)
113	
114	theorem IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing_types (h : F.IsSheaf) (sf : ∀ i : ι, F.obj (op (U i)))
115	    (cpt : IsCompatible F U sf) : ∃! s : F.obj (op (iSup U)), IsGluing F U sf s := by
116	  simp_rw [isGluing_iff_pairwise]
117	  exact (Types.isLimit_iff _).mp (h.isSheafPairwiseIntersections U) _ cpt.sectionPairwise.prop

123	usual sheaf condition.
124	-/

        (g : Uo ⟶ Vo) (hV : Vo.unop ≠ ⊥)
        (x : ↥(lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf P hPcoh Uo)),
        (((lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf P hPcoh).map g).hom x).1 = x.1 := by
      intros Uo Vo g hV x
      simp only [lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf, dif_neg hV,
        ModuleCat.hom_ofHom]
      rfl

    have hexists_ne_bot : ∃ i, U i ≠ ⊥ := by
      by_contra h
      push_neg at h
      exact hSup (iSup_eq_bot.mpr h)
    obtain ⟨i₀, hUi₀⟩ := hexists_ne_bot
    -- Candidate value v ∈ K(C).
    set v : C.left.functionField := (sf i₀).1 with hv_def

    have hIrr : IrreducibleSpace ↑C.left := inferInstance
    have hPre : PreirreducibleSpace ↑C.left := hIrr.toPreirreducibleSpace
    have inter_ne_bot : ∀ i j, U i ≠ ⊥ → U j ≠ ⊥ →
        (U i ⊓ U j : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) ≠ ⊥ := by
      intro i j hi hj h
      apply (TopologicalSpace.Opens.not_nonempty_iff_eq_bot _).mpr h
      have ni : (U i : Set _).Nonempty := by
        by_contra hh
        exact hi ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.not_nonempty_iff_eq_bot _).mp hh)
      have nj : (U j : Set _).Nonempty := by
        by_contra hh
        exact hj ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.not_nonempty_iff_eq_bot _).mp hh)
      exact nonempty_preirreducible_inter (U i).isOpen (U j).isOpen ni nj

    have key_val : ∀ i, U i ≠ ⊥ → (sf i).1 = v := by
      intro i hUi
      have hint : (U i ⊓ U i₀ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) ≠ ⊥ :=
        inter_ne_bot i i₀ hUi hUi₀
      have hc := hcompat i i₀
      have h1 : (((lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf P hPcoh).map
            (CategoryTheory.infLELeft (U i) (U i₀)).op).hom (sf i)).1 = (sf i).1 :=
        map_val _ hint (sf i)
      have h2 : (((lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf P hPcoh).map
            (CategoryTheory.infLERight (U i) (U i₀)).op).hom (sf i₀)).1 = (sf i₀).1 :=
        map_val _ hint (sf i₀)
      have hc_val := congr_arg Subtype.val hc
      simp only at hc_val
      rw [h1, h2] at hc_val
      exact hc_val

    have hv_mem : v ∈ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf P hPcoh
        (Opposite.op (iSup U)) := by
      refine ⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, Or.inl hSup⟩
      · -- ord_Q(v) ≥ 0 for Q.point ∈ iSup U, Q.point ≠ P.
        intro Q hQ hQP
        obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp hQ
        have hUi : U i ≠ ⊥ := by
          intro hh
          rw [hh] at hi
          exact (TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_bot.mp hi).elim
        have : (sf i).1 = v := key_val i hUi
        rw [← this]
        exact (sf i).2.1.1 Q hi hQP
      · -- ord_P(v) ≥ -1 when P ∈ iSup U.
        intro hP
        obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp hP
        have hUi : U i ≠ ⊥ := by
          intro hh
          rw [hh] at hi
          exact (TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_bot.mp hi).elim
        have : (sf i).1 = v := key_val i hUi
        rw [← this]
        exact (sf i).2.1.2 hi
    -- Build the gluing element and verify uniqueness.
    refine ⟨⟨v, hv_mem⟩, ?_, ?_⟩
    · -- IsGluing: ∀ i, F.map _ ⟨v, hv_mem⟩ = sf i.
      intro i
      apply Subtype.ext
      by_cases hUi : U i = ⊥
      · -- U i = ⊥: image's .1 is 0 (codomain is ↥⊥); also (sf i).1 = 0.
        have hsfi : (sf i).1 = 0 := hSubAt0 (U i) hUi (sf i)
        change (((lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf P hPcoh).map
          (CategoryTheory.homOfLE (le_iSup U i)).op).hom ⟨v, hv_mem⟩).1 = (sf i).1
        rw [hsfi]
        exact hSubAt0 (U i) hUi _
      · -- U i ≠ ⊥: image's .1 = v = (sf i).1.
        change (((lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf P hPcoh).map
          (CategoryTheory.homOfLE (le_iSup U i)).op).hom ⟨v, hv_mem⟩).1 = (sf i).1
        rw [map_val _ hUi ⟨v, hv_mem⟩, key_val i hUi]

      intro s' hgluing
      apply Subtype.ext
      have hi₀g := hgluing i₀
      have h_apply :
          (((lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf P hPcoh).map
              (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i₀).op).hom s').1 = s'.1 :=
        map_val _ hUi₀ s'
      have := congr_arg Subtype.val hi₀g
      simp only at this
      rw [h_apply] at this
      exact this

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Forget
9	public import Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.SheafCondition.PairwiseIntersections
10	
11	/-!
12	# The sheaf condition in terms of unique gluings
13	
14	We provide an alternative formulation of the sheaf condition in terms of unique gluings.
15	
16	We work with sheaves valued in a concrete category `C` admitting all limits, whose forgetful
17	functor `C ⥤ Type` preserves limits and reflects isomorphisms. The usual categories of algebraic
18	structures, such as `MonCat`, `AddCommGrpCat`, `RingCat`, `CommRingCat` etc. are all examples of
19	this kind of category.
20	
21	A presheaf `F : Presheaf C X` satisfies the sheaf condition if and only if, for every
22	compatible family of sections `sf : Π i : ι, F.obj (op (U i))`, there exists a unique gluing
23	`s : F.obj (op (iSup U))`.
24	
25	Here, the family `sf` is called compatible, if for all `i j : ι`, the restrictions of `sf i`
26	and `sf j` to `U i ⊓ U j` agree. A section `s : F.obj (op (iSup U))` is a gluing for the
27	family `sf`, if `s` restricts to `sf i` on `U i` for all `i : ι`
28	
29	We show that the sheaf condition in terms of unique gluings is equivalent to the definition
30	in terms of pairwise intersections. Our approach is as follows: First, we show them to be equivalent
31	for `Type`-valued presheaves. Then we use that composing a presheaf with a limit-preserving and
32	isomorphism-reflecting functor leaves the sheaf condition invariant, as shown in
33	`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Forget.lean`.
34	
35	-/
36	
37	@[expose] public section
38	
39	noncomputable section
40	
41	open TopCat TopCat.Presheaf CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits
42	  TopologicalSpace TopologicalSpace.Opens Opposite
43	
44	universe x
45	
46	variable {C : Type*} [Category* C] {FC : C → C → Type*} {CC : C → Type*}
47	variable [∀ X Y, FunLike (FC X Y) (CC X) (CC Y)] [ConcreteCategory C FC]
48	
49	namespace TopCat
50	
51	namespace Presheaf
52	
53	section
54	
55	variable {X : TopCat.{x}} (F : Presheaf C X) {ι : Type*} (U : ι → Opens X)
56	
57	/-- A family of sections `sf` is compatible, if the restrictions of `sf i` and `sf j` to `U i ⊓ U j`
58	agree, for all `i` and `j`
59	-/
60	def IsCompatible (sf : ∀ i : ι, ToType (F.obj (op (U i)))) : Prop :=
61	  ∀ i j : ι, F.map (infLELeft (U i) (U j)).op (sf i) = F.map (infLERight (U i) (U j)).op (sf j)
62	
63	/-- A section `s` is a gluing for a family of sections `sf` if it restricts to `sf i` on `U i`,
64	for all `i`
65	-/
66	def IsGluing (sf : ∀ i : ι, ToType (F.obj (op (U i)))) (s : ToType (F.obj (op (iSup U)))) : Prop :=
67	  ∀ i : ι, F.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op s = sf i
68	
69	/--
70	The sheaf condition in terms of unique gluings. A presheaf `F : Presheaf C X` satisfies this sheaf
71	condition if and only if, for every compatible family of sections `sf : Π i : ι, F.obj (op (U i))`,
72	there exists a unique gluing `s : F.obj (op (iSup U))`.
73	
74	We prove this to be equivalent to the usual one below in
75	`TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheafUniqueGluing`

# AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean — iter-189 Lane A

## Summary

**HARD BAR MET**: Case B sorry in `carrierPresheaf_isSheaf` closed.
**Net sorry count**: 4 → 3 (−1).
**Helper budget used**: 1 (`map_val` inline helper).
**Effort**: ~100 LOC substantive proof body.

## `carrierPresheaf_isSheaf` Case B (line ~644, was sorry at L686)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach**: Direct unique-gluing argument via irreducibility,
  rather than routing through `Subfunctor.isSheaf_iff`. The
  `carrierTypeSubfunctor` (iter-189 refactor) is in scope but not
  required by the proof — the underlying observation (constant
  function gluing via irreducibility) translates cleanly to direct
  use of the ambient `IsSheafUniqueGluing` form.
- **Result**: RESOLVED.
- **Key steps** (all axiom-clean modulo pre-existing WeilDivisor.lean
  sorries on `Scheme.RationalMap.order` / `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`):

  1. **Helper `map_val`**: when target `Vo.unop ≠ ⊥`, the
     `carrierPresheaf.map` is `Submodule.inclusion`, preserving the
     `.1` underlying value. Closed via `simp only [carrierPresheaf,
     dif_neg hV, ModuleCat.hom_ofHom]; rfl`.

  2. **Non-empty witness extraction**: from `iSup U ≠ ⊥`, get
     `∃ i₀, U i₀ ≠ ⊥` via `iSup_eq_bot` contrapositive.

  3. **Irreducibility-driven non-empty intersection**: from
     `IsIntegral C.left ⟹ IrreducibleSpace ↥C.left
     ⟹ PreirreducibleSpace ↥C.left`, then
     `nonempty_preirreducible_inter` gives `U i ⊓ U j ≠ ⊥` for any
     non-empty `U i`, `U j`. NOTE: `nonempty_preirreducible_inter`
     needed the `@`-form to bridge the `↥C.left` vs
     `↑C.left.toTopCat` instance friction (defeq but not
     instance-resolvable by `infer_instance`).

  4. **Value uniformity** `key_val : ∀ i, U i ≠ ⊥ → (sf i).1 = v`:
     compatibility `hcompat i i₀` projected to `.1` via `congr_arg
     Subtype.val`, then `rw [map_val (sf i)] [map_val (sf i₀)]`.

  5. **Membership** `hv_mem : v ∈ carrierSubmoduleSheaf (op (iSup U))`:
     `trivAtBot` via `Or.inl hSup`; order conditions via
     `TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup` ↦ pick `i` with `Q.point ∈ U i`
     ↦ transfer via `(sf i).2.1.1` / `(sf i).2.1.2` and `key_val`.

  6. **IsGluing**: for `U i = ⊥`, both sides have `.1 = 0` via
     `hSubAt0`; for `U i ≠ ⊥`, `map_val + key_val` close.

  7. **Uniqueness**: `s'.1 = v` via `gluing i₀ ↦ map_val` extraction.

- **Lemmas used (all Mathlib)**:
  - `iSup_eq_bot` (CompleteLattice basic).
  - `IrreducibleSpace.toPreirreducibleSpace`.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.irreducibleSpace_of_isIntegral` (instance).
  - `nonempty_preirreducible_inter` (Topology/Irreducible).
  - `TopologicalSpace.Opens.not_nonempty_iff_eq_bot`.
  - `TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup`.
  - `TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_bot`.
  - `TopologicalSpace.Opens.infLELeft` / `infLERight`.
  - `Submodule.inclusion`.
  - `ModuleCat.hom_ofHom`.

## Remaining sorries (NOT in iter-189 scope)

3 sorries remain at L1154, L1191, L1249:

- `h1_vanishing_genusZero` (L1154): requires LES of cohomology +
  skyscraper sheaf vanishing + `g(C) = 0`. Out of scope for iter-189
  Lane A directive ("Subfunctor refactor → Case B close").

- `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` (L1191): cascading from h1_vanishing +
  `RR.2` Euler-characteristic identity. Out of scope.

- `exists_nonconstant_genusZero` (L1249): cascading from
  dim_eq_two + linear algebra. Out of scope.

These were not part of the iter-189 plan agent's directive for this
file (per `iter/iter-189/objectives.md` Lane A).

## Blueprint markers

The blueprint chapter `chapters/RiemannRoch_OCofP.tex` already has
`\leanok` on the statement of `lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_carrierPresheaf_isSheaf`
(via iter-187 plan-phase pin). With Case B now closed, this lemma
is fully formalized (modulo the transitive `Ring.ordFrac_*` substrate
which is project-side substrate, not Mathlib gap). The
`sync_leanok` phase will deterministically add `\leanok` to the
proof block based on actual sorry count in the body — no action
needed from the prover.

## Notes

- The Subfunctor restructure landed by the iter-189 refactor agent
  (`carrierTypeSubfunctor` def at L439, `hsub_mem` lemma in Case B
  at L659) was inspectable but unused: the irreducibility-based
  direct argument was cleaner than routing through
  `Subfunctor.isSheaf_iff` because the carrier presheaf is
  `ModuleCat`-valued (not `Type`-valued), and the Subfunctor
  framework would have required an additional translation step.
  The `carrierTypeSubfunctor` def remains in the file as
  documentation of the conceptual approach.

- The proof closed axiom-clean modulo pre-existing sorryAx
  transitive dependencies via `Scheme.RationalMap.order`
  (WeilDivisor.lean L231, L463). No new project axioms introduced.
