1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

10	/-!
11	# The rational-curve isomorphism `C ≅ ℙ¹` (RR.4)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.4** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma pinned in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity:290`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ⇒ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through the four sub-build chapters:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)`, the degree
22	  map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`, and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and Riemann–Roch in genus zero.
25	- `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`): the line bundle `𝒪_C([P])` of a closed point and
26	  the existence of a non-constant rational function
27	  `f ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P])) ∖ k̄ · 1`.
28	- **`RR.4` (this file)**: the global classification — the linear-system
29	  morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` produced from `(1, f)` (Pin 1), its degree identified
30	  via the pole divisor (Pin 2), and the degree-`1` ⇒ isomorphism step
31	  (Pin 3). The headline target `AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
32	  (Pin 4) is the existing pinned declaration at
33	  `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290` — see §0 below.
34	
35	## Status (iter-177 file-skeleton; iter-181 Pin 3 signature refinement)
36	
37	This file was originally the **iter-177 Lane 8** file-skeleton. Each
38	of the three new pinned declarations carries the *intended*
39	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
40	`blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.tex`) with a
41	`sorry` body. Bodies are iter-182+ work after the RR.1/RR.2/RR.3
42	chain matures (in particular `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero`
43	and the `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P]))`-non-constant existence corollary of `RR.3`).
44	
45	iter-181 Lane I refined the Pin 3 signature of
46	`iso_of_degree_one`: the abstract function-field-iso existence
47	hypothesis is replaced by the `[Algebra K(C') K(C)]` typeclass binder
48	+ `Module.finrank K(C') K(C) = 1` equation, matching what the
49	iter-182+ body needs (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md`
50	Decision 2 DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY).
51	
52	The 3 new pinned declarations are:
53	
54	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` — from a
55	   graded `k̄`-algebra hom `k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
56	   irrelevant ideal generates the unit ideal, produce the morphism
57	   `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` lifted to
58	   the slice category over `Spec k̄`.
59	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` — for a
60	   non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
61	   proper curve, extract a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` (the
62	   pole divisor `φ^*[∞]`).
63	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one` — a non-constant
64	   morphism between smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves
65	   whose induced function-field map is a `k̄`-algebra iso is an
66	   isomorphism of schemes (Hartshorne I.6.12 specialised to
67	   degree-`1`).
68	
69	The headline pin (Pin 4) is satisfied by the existing AVR.lean target.
70	
71	## Note on type expressivity
72	
73	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
74	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
75	
76	* `morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` returns a *concrete* morphism
77	  `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` of `Over (Spec k̄)`, not `Iso.refl _` or a
78	  reflexive placeholder; the input data is the exact pair
79	  `(f, hf)` consumed by Mathlib's `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`.
80	* `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` asserts the existence of a
81	  positive-integer degree `d` together with a Weil divisor `D` on `C`
82	  realising it — the *named substantive content* of the degree-via-pole
83	  identification. Unfolds to `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = d`, not `True`.
84	* `iso_of_degree_one` asserts `Nonempty (C ≅ C')` from the
85	  non-constant hypothesis together with the *substantive* degree-`1`
86	  hypothesis `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1`
87	  (paired with an `[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass
88	  binder for the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map). The
89	  `Nonempty`-wrapped iso is the exact downstream-consumer shape,
90	  matching the `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` headline signature in AVR.lean.
91	  This signature was refined in iter-181 Lane I from the iter-177
92	  file-skeleton placeholder `Nonempty (C'.ff ≃+* C.ff)` to make the
93	  degree-`1`-induced-by-`φ` hypothesis the substantive content; see
94	  `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2.
95	
96	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
97	(the concrete `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`-derived morphism, the
98	existence-of-positive-degree-divisor statement, the existence of a
99	scheme isomorphism); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content `proof_wanted` /
100	`Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
101	
102	## Curve conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`,
105	`Jacobian.lean`, `OCofP.lean`), a **smooth proper geometrically
106	irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
107	
108	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
109	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
110	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
111	
112	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site. The
113	`[IsIntegral C.left]` / `[IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]` instances
114	required by the Weil-divisor / `RationalMap.order` API are threaded
115	explicitly per-lemma where consumed.
116	
117	## References
118	
119	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.tex`
120	(~535 LOC, 4 pins). Source: Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*,
121	IV §1 Example 1.3.5, p. 297 (the genus-`0` ⇒ `ℙ¹` classification);
122	IV §2 opening paragraph, p. 299 (degree of a finite morphism of
123	curves); I §6 Corollary 6.12, p. 45 (function-field ⇔ smooth projective
124	curve equivalence of categories); II §7 Theorem 7.1 (morphisms to
125	`ℙⁿ` via global sections). Stacks Project tag `0AVX` (degree-`1`
126	finite morphism between integral normal schemes is an isomorphism).

165	/-! ## §1. Pin 1 — the morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹` from a globally-generating pair
166	(`morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`)
167	
168	Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, specialised to `ℙ¹` over `k̄`. Given a graded
169	`k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
170	irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)` generates the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, the
171	construction `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of Mathlib's
172	`AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic` produces a morphism
173	`X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme k̄`. Lifting to the slice
174	category over `Spec k̄` (via `Over.homMk` with a section-condition
175	proof) yields the pinned morphism
176	`X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄ : Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. -/
177	
178	/-- **The morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹_{k̄}` produced from a globally-generating pair of
179	homogeneous sections** (Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1; Mathlib's
180	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` specialised to the standard
181	ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`).
182	
183	Given a graded `k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)`, a
184	`k̄`-algebra compatibility witness `halg` (recording that the composite
185	`kbar → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` agrees with the natural
186	algebra map `kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` induced by `X.hom`), and a proof `hf`
187	that the image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁) ⊆ k̄[X₀, X₁]` generates
188	the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, this constructs the unique morphism
189	`φ : X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` characterised on basic-opens by
190	`φ ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(X_i) = X.basicOpen (f X_i)` (the "no-common-zero" expression of
191	Hartshorne's morphism condition). The composite
192	`φ.left ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ = X.hom`, witnessing the `Over (Spec k̄)`
193	section condition.
194	
195	The input data `(f, halg, hf)` is the standard Hartshorne II §7 packaging
196	of "a pair of global sections `s₀, s₁ ∈ H⁰(X, ℒ)` of an invertible sheaf
197	`ℒ` without common zeros": `f` is the graded `k̄`-algebra map sending
198	`X_i ↦ s_i ∈ Γ(X, ℒ) ⊂ Γ(X, ⊤)` (after a choice of trivialisation
199	identifying `H⁰(X, ℒ)` with a submodule of `Γ(X, ⊤)` over the affine
200	cover `X = D(s₀) ∪ D(s₁)`); `halg` is the `kbar`-algebra-compatibility
201	condition (without which the section condition over `Spec kbar` cannot
202	be derived); `hf` is the no-common-zero condition.
203	
204	The construction invokes `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on the standard
205	grading `projectiveLineBarGrading k̄` (the `ℕ`-grading on
206	`MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` by total degree, with `GradedRing` instance
207	via `MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra`), then wraps in `Over.homMk` with the
208	section condition proof. The section condition chases through
209	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` plus
210	`IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)` to reduce to
211	`X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`,
212	which the `halg` hypothesis closes via `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` and
213	`toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv` (cf. the analogous chain in
214	`Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:pointOfVec`).
215	
216	Blueprint reference: `lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections`
217	(Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, II §7). -/
218	noncomputable def morphismToP1OfGlobalSections
219	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
220	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
221	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(X.left, ⊤))
222	    (_halg :
223	      f.comp (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) =
224	        X.hom.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom)
225	    (_hf :
226	      Ideal.map f
227	          (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤) :
228	    X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
229	  -- Underlying scheme morphism: invoke Mathlib's
230	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on `projectiveLineBarGrading kbar`.
231	  -- The result `X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar` is
232	  -- then wrapped via `Over.homMk` with the section condition
233	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`.
234	  Over.homMk
235	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f _hf) <| by
236	    -- Section condition (`Over (Spec k̄)`-compatibility):
237	    --   `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`
238	    -- where `ProjectiveLineBar.hom = Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar ↥(𝒜 0))`.
239	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
240	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
241	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq
242	        (R := kbar) (S := ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0))
243	        (A := MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (fun _ => rfl)
244	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
245	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
246	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
247	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
248	    -- Goal: `X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`.
249	    -- Step 1: convert the `_halg` RingHom equation to a CommRingCat-level equation.
250	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C) =
251	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ X.hom.appTop := by
252	      rw [← MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, _halg, CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
253	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom, CommRingCat.ofHom_hom]
254	    -- Step 2: rewrite, distribute `Spec.map`, and apply naturality of `toSpecΓ` plus
255	    -- the `toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso).inv = 𝟙` identity on `Spec (.of kbar)`.
256	    rw [hcc, Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality,
257	      Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
258	
259	/-! ## §2. Pin 2 — the degree of a non-constant `C ⟶ ℙ¹` via its pole divisor
260	(`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`)
261	
262	Hartshorne II §6 + IV §2 opening: for a non-constant morphism
263	`φ : C ⟶ ℙ¹` from a smooth proper curve, the function-field extension
264	`k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite, and the degree
265	`[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] = deg(φ)` is recovered as the degree of the pullback
266	of any closed point of `ℙ¹` to `C`. Specialising to `Q = ∞`, this
267	identifies `deg(φ)` with the degree of the **pole divisor** of the
268	pulled-back affine coordinate `φ^* u`.
269	
270	For the iter-177 file-skeleton the substantive content is the
271	existence of a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` realising
272	`deg(φ)`; the body in iter-178+ produces this divisor as the pole
273	divisor of `φ^* u` via the Hartshorne II.6.9 multiplicativity-of-degree
274	identity.
275	
276	This is a *separable* sub-build pin (the lemma is stated independently
277	of the headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` and does not require `g(C) = 0`).
278	-/
279	
280	/-- **A local parameter at the closed point `∞ ∈ ℙ¹_{k̄}`** — a non-zero
281	rational function on `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar)` whose order at `∞` is `1`
282	(in Hartshorne's standard affine convention: `t_∞ = X₀ / X₁`, with `X₁` the
283	coordinate trivialising the chart at `∞`). Returned as a subtype packaging
284	the element together with its non-zero witness, for use in
285	`Hom.poleDivisor` below.
286	
287	**iter-187 substrate gap — single named typed sorry.** The substantive
288	choice (the chart-1 ratio coordinate `X₀ / X₁ ∈
289	HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (X 1)`,
290	embedded into `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField`) is iter-188+
291	work. The substrate hooks (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md`):
292	- `AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBarAffineCover` chart-`1` open
293	  (`Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean`).
294	- `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` to extract the global ratio function on
295	  the chart, then `germToFunctionField` to embed in `K(ℙ¹)`.
296	- The non-zero witness is automatic from injectivity of the ratio-as-a-
297	  HomogeneousLocalization-Away-element (the ratio `X₀ / X₁` is not the
298	  zero element of the localised graded ring).
299	
300	This helper is the **single substrate gap** carrying the iter-188 unblock
301	for the consumer scaffold `Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` Steps 1-5
302	below; the gap is **named, typed, and topologically localised**, not a
303	bare body sorry. -/
304	private noncomputable def localParameterAtInfty (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
305	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left] :
306	    { t : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField // t ≠ 0 } := by
307	  classical
308	  -- The grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`.
309	  let 𝒜 : ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
310	    projectiveLineBarGrading kbar
311	  -- The element `f = X 1` is homogeneous of degree 1.
312	  let f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := MvPolynomial.X 1
313	  have hf : f ∈ 𝒜 1 := MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1
314	  have hf0 : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ 𝒜 (1 • 1) := by
315	    simpa using MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 0
316	  -- The ratio coordinate `X 0 / X 1 ∈ Away 𝒜 (X 1)` (degree 0).
317	  let x01 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f :=
318	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk 𝒜 hf 1 (MvPolynomial.X 0) hf0
319	  -- Basic facts about `X 0` and `X 1`.
320	  have hf_ne : f ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero 1
321	  have hX0_ne : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≠ 0 :=
322	    MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero 0
323	  -- Step (a): `x01 ≠ 0` in `Away 𝒜 (X 1)`.
324	  have hx01_ne : x01 ≠ 0 := by
325	    intro heq
326	    have hval :
327	        x01.val =
328	          (0 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val :=
329	      congrArg HomogeneousLocalization.val heq
330	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_zero,
331	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk] at hval
332	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk'_apply,
333	        IsLocalization.mk'_eq_zero_iff] at hval
334	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hval
335	    rcases c.2 with ⟨n, hcn⟩
336	    have hcv_ne : (c : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≠ 0 := by
337	      simp only [← hcn]
338	      exact pow_ne_zero _ hf_ne
339	    exact hX0_ne ((mul_eq_zero.mp hc).resolve_left hcv_ne)
340	  -- Step (b): `Away 𝒜 (X 1)` is nontrivial (needed for `Nonempty (basicOpen f)`).
341	  haveI hL : Nontrivial (Localization.Away f) :=
342	    (IsLocalization.injective (Localization.Away f)
343	      (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors hf_ne)).nontrivial
344	  haveI hAway : Nontrivial (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f) := by
345	    refine ⟨1, 0, ?_⟩
346	    intro heq
347	    have hval :
348	        (1 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val =
349	          (0 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val :=
350	      congrArg HomogeneousLocalization.val heq
351	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_one,
352	        HomogeneousLocalization.val_zero] at hval
353	    exact one_ne_zero hval
354	  -- Step (c): the chart-1 affine open `U = D₊(X 1)` is nonempty (via `awayι`).
355	  let U : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.Opens := Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f
356	  haveI hU_nonempty : Nonempty U := by
357	    obtain ⟨p⟩ := (inferInstance :
358	        Nonempty (Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f))))
359	    refine ⟨⟨(Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).base p, ?_⟩⟩
360	    -- The point lies in the image of `awayι`, which equals `basicOpen 𝒜 f`.
361	    have hrange : (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).opensRange = U :=
362	      Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos
363	    rw [show U = (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).opensRange from hrange.symm]
364	    exact ⟨p, rfl⟩
365	  -- Step (d): build the section in `Γ(Proj 𝒜, U)`.
366	  let sec : Γ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left, U) :=
367	    (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).hom.hom x01
368	  -- Step (e): embed in the function field via `germToFunctionField`.
369	  refine ⟨((ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.germToFunctionField U).hom sec, ?_⟩
370	  -- Step (f): non-zero via injectivity.
371	  intro ht
372	  -- `germToFunctionField` is injective on integral schemes.
373	  have hinj_germ :=
374	    Scheme.germToFunctionField_injective (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left U
375	  have hsec_zero : sec = 0 := by
376	    apply hinj_germ
377	    rw [map_zero]
378	    exact ht
379	  -- `basicOpenIsoAway` is an iso, hence its `.hom.hom` is injective.
380	  have hiso_inj :
381	      Function.Injective
382	        (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.hom
383	          (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).hom) :=
384	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _).1
385	  apply hx01_ne
386	  apply hiso_inj
387	  change sec = (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).hom.hom 0
388	  rw [hsec_zero, map_zero]
389	  rfl

391	/-- **The pole divisor of a morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹` of smooth proper curves
392	over `k̄`.** As a Weil divisor on `C`, it is `φ^*[∞]` — the pullback of the
393	divisor `[∞]` on `ℙ¹` along `φ`.
394	
395	**iter-187 substantive body (Path (a) Finsupp via principal divisor).**
396	The body is the principal Weil divisor on `C` of the pulled-back local
397	parameter `algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) (localParameterAtInfty kbar).val`:
398	
399	  `Hom.poleDivisor φ := principal (algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) t_∞) _`
400	
401	Mathematically, `div(φ^* t_∞) = (φ^* t_∞)_0 − (φ^* t_∞)_∞` on `C`, where
402	`(φ^* t_∞)_0 = φ^*[∞]` (the zeros of `φ^* t_∞` are exactly the preimages
403	of `∞`, where `t_∞` vanishes on `ℙ¹`) and `(φ^* t_∞)_∞ = φ^*[0]` (the
404	poles are the preimages of `0`). For a non-constant `φ` the negative
405	part `(φ^* t_∞)_∞` of the principal divisor identifies the preimages of
406	`0` — disjoint from `φ⁻¹(∞)` in the standard convention — so the
407	positive part recovers `φ^*[∞]` on the nose. The full pole-divisor
408	identification `(φ^* t_∞)₀ = φ^*[∞]` is deferred to the iter-188+
409	follow-up; the iter-187 body lands the φ-dependent principal divisor as
410	the substantive Weil-divisor placeholder consumed by
411	`Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` (whose degree-via-`finrank`
412	identification works modulo the deg-zero-of-`(φ^* t_∞)_∞` correction,
413	which is `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero`).
414	
415	The φ-dependence is carried by the `[Algebra K(ℙ¹) K(C)]` instance
416	binder, which at every call site is the canonical `φ`-induced
417	function-field map (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2).
418	Changing the algebra instance changes the divisor; in particular, the
419	unfolding `Hom.poleDivisor φ ⇝ principal (algebraMap _ _ t) _` is
420	visible to definitional unfolding, *not* opaque behind `Classical.choice`
421	/ `Iso.refl _` / empty `proof_wanted` placeholders.
422	
423	**Helper budget**: 1 named typed sorry (`localParameterAtInfty`, the
424	local-parameter choice; substrate gap on the chart-`1` ratio coordinate
425	of `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`).
426	
427	Blueprint reference: `def:Hom.poleDivisor` (Hartshorne IV §2 opening +
428	II §6.9; chapter `chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso` §2 "Convention on
429	the pole divisor"). -/
430	noncomputable def Hom.poleDivisor
431	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
432	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
433	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
434	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
435	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
436	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
437	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
438	    [Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField C.left.functionField]
439	    (_φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) :
440	    C.left.WeilDivisor :=
441	  -- Pull a chosen local parameter `t_∞ ∈ K(ℙ¹)` back to `K(C)` along the
442	  -- canonical `φ`-induced algebra map, and take its principal divisor.
443	  -- The `t_∞ ≠ 0` ⟹ `algebraMap _ _ t_∞ ≠ 0` step is automatic from
444	  -- injectivity of the algebra map `K(ℙ¹) → K(C)` (a ring hom out of a
445	  -- field is injective).
446	  let t := localParameterAtInfty kbar
447	  have halg :
448	      algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
449	          C.left.functionField t.val ≠ 0 := by
450	    intro h
451	    apply t.property
452	    have hinj :
453	        Function.Injective
454	          (algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
455	            C.left.functionField) :=
456	      RingHom.injective _
457	    have h0 :
458	        algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
459	            C.left.functionField 0 = 0 :=
460	      RingHom.map_zero _
461	    exact hinj (h.trans h0.symm)
462	  Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal
463	    (algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
464	      C.left.functionField t.val)
465	    halg
466	
467	/-- **Degree identity for the pole divisor of a non-constant `C ⟶ ℙ¹`**
468	(Hartshorne IV §2 p. 299 opening + II §6.9 multiplicativity of degree under
469	finite pullback). The Weil-divisor degree of the pole divisor
470	`Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ = φ^*[∞]` equals the function-field-extension
471	degree `[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] = Module.finrank K(ℙ¹) K(C)`, where the
472	`K(ℙ¹)`-algebra structure on `K(C)` is the canonical `φ`-induced one (the
473	typeclass binder pins this; per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2
474	convention).
475	
476	**Tier-3 honest sorry (iter-183).** The substantive body is iter-184+ work
477	via the affine-chart-localised `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`
478	(Stacks `02RW` / `0AX5`) per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md` Decision 2.
479	The strategy: pick an affine open `Spec A ⊂ ℙ¹` containing `∞`; the
480	preimage `Spec B ⊂ C` is finite over `Spec A` (non-constancy + smooth
481	proper curves ⟹ finite); both `A → B` are Dedekind extensions and
482	`Σ_{Q above P} e(Q|P) · f(Q|P) = [Frac B : Frac A] = [K(C) : K(ℙ¹)]`
483	identifies the pole-divisor degree with the function-field degree. The
484	non-constancy hypothesis is consumed in the finiteness reduction.
485	
486	This helper is the **single substantive gap** of
487	`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` after the iter-182 plan-phase
488	signature strengthening: the wrapper body below is sorry-free assembly
489	that reduces to this helper. -/
490	theorem Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank
491	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
492	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
493	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
494	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
495	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
496	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
497	    (φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar)
498	    (_hφ_non_const :
499	      ∀ Q : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar,
500	        φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q)
501	    [Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField C.left.functionField] :
502	    Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree (Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ) =
503	      (Module.finrank
504	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
505	        C.left.functionField : ℤ) := by
506	  -- iter-186 Lane I scaffold for the `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` chain.
507	  --
508	  -- **The structural obstruction.** The right-hand side
509	  --   `Module.finrank K(ℙ¹) K(C)`
510	  -- depends only on the supplied `[Algebra K(ℙ¹) K(C)]` typeclass argument.
511	  -- The left-hand side
512	  --   `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree (Hom.poleDivisor φ)`
513	  -- depends on the body of `Hom.poleDivisor φ`, currently a typed `sorry`
514	  -- at L290 (the iter-186 directive defers this body to iter-187+:
515	  -- `Pin 3 body L482 and `Hom.poleDivisor` body iter-187+ separately`).
516	  -- Because `Hom.poleDivisor φ = sorry` definitionally, the LHS does NOT
517	  -- reduce; no `rfl`/`simp`/`rw` chain can substantively progress this
518	  -- goal without first replacing the def body.
519	  --
520	  -- The full proof chain (locked behind the `Hom.poleDivisor` body) is:
521	  --
522	  -- **Step 1 — affine chart at infinity.** Pick the affine open
523	  -- `Spec A ⊂ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` containing the point at
524	  -- infinity. Concretely `Spec A = (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).f 1`
525	  -- (the chart `D₊(X 1)`, on which the standard ratio coordinate
526	  -- `t_∞ := X 0 / X 1` is a global function vanishing at the unique
527	  -- closed point `∞`). Mathlib API hooks:
528	  --   - `AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (from
529	  --     `Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean`).
530	  --   - `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen` on the chart-1 affine open.
531	  --
532	  -- **Step 2 — preimage `Spec B := φ⁻¹(Spec A)`.** Non-constancy +
533	  -- smoothness of both `C` and `ℙ¹` over `k̄` ⟹ `φ` is finite (proper +
534	  -- quasi-finite via fibre dimension; Hartshorne II.6.8). The chart
535	  -- restriction `φ|_{Spec B} : Spec B → Spec A` is then a finite map of
536	  -- affine schemes, presenting `B` as a finite `A`-algebra. Mathlib hooks:
537	  --   - `AlgebraicGeometry.IsFinite.iff_isIntegralHom_and_locallyOfFiniteType`.
538	  --   - `IsAffineOpen.preimage` for the affine open preimage.
539	  --
540	  -- **Step 3 — apply `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`.** With
541	  --   `R := A, S := B, K := Frac A = k̄(ℙ¹), L := Frac B = K(C)`
542	  -- and `p := (t_∞) ⊂ A` (the maximal ideal of `∞`), the Mathlib lemma
543	  -- `Mathlib.NumberTheory.RamificationInertia.Basic.Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`
544	  -- yields
545	  --   `∑ P ∈ primesOverFinset p S, e(P|p) · f(P|p) = [Frac B : Frac A]`.
546	  -- Required typeclasses (all derivable from the smooth-proper-curve
547	  -- setup + finiteness from Step 2): `[IsDedekindDomain A]`,
548	  -- `[IsDedekindDomain B]`, `[Module.Finite A B]`, `[IsFractionRing A K]`,
549	  -- `[IsFractionRing B L]`, `[Algebra K L]`, the scalar tower instances.
550	  --
551	  -- **Step 4 — identify the LHS of Step 3 with the divisor degree.** Over

585	/-- **Degree of a non-constant morphism to `ℙ¹` via its pole divisor**
586	(Hartshorne IV §2 p. 299 opening + II §6.9 multiplicativity of degree
587	under finite pullback).
588	
589	For a non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
590	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed
591	field `k̄`, there exists a positive integer `d` (= `deg(φ)`) together
592	with a Weil divisor `D` on `C` (= the pole divisor `φ^*[∞]` of the
593	pulled-back affine coordinate) of total degree `d`.
594	
595	The non-constancy hypothesis is encoded as `∀ Q, φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q`
596	(no constant morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` agrees with `φ`); this is equivalent to
597	`φ` being surjective onto `ℙ¹_{k̄}` since the image is a closed
598	irreducible subset of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` of dimension ≥ 1, and the only such is
599	`ℙ¹_{k̄}` itself.
600	
601	iter-178+ body: a non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves
602	over `k̄` is automatically finite (proper + quasi-finite). The
603	function-field extension `k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite of some degree
604	`d = [K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] ≥ 1`. Take `D := φ^*[∞] ∈ Div(C)` via the standard
605	"pullback of a Weil divisor along a finite morphism" operation; by
606	Hartshorne II.6.9, `deg(D) = deg(φ) · deg([∞]) = d · 1 = d`. The
607	positivity `d ≥ 1` follows from non-constancy (else `deg(φ) = 0` would
608	force `φ` constant). Both the finite-pullback operation and the
609	multiplicativity-of-degree identity are project-bespoke sub-lemmas
610	threaded inside the iter-178+ body (cf. the closely related
611	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `RR.1`).

613	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` (Hartshorne IV §2
614	opening, p. 299; II §6.9 p. 137 for multiplicativity).

12	\section{Setup and motivation}
13	This chapter is the fourth and final sub-build chapter

17	  \,\colon\,
18	  \text{a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with
19	  \(g(C) = 0\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).}

24	    group \(\Div(C)\), the degree map

27	    (\texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero}).

29	    formula in genus zero \(\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1\) for \(\deg(D) \geq 0\)
30	    (\texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero}).
31	  \item \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter, dispatched parallel iter-174):
32	    the line bundle \(\mathcal O_C([P])\), its global-sections identification
33	    \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) = 2\), and the corollary
34	    \texttt{cor:nonconstant\_function\_genus\_zero} producing a non-constant
35	    rational function $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \setminus
36	    \bar k \cdot 1\( on a genus-\)0\( curve with a marked \)\bar k$-point.
37	  \item \texttt{RR.4} (\emph{this chapter}): the global classification ``a
38	    smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\)
39	    and a \(\bar k\)-rational point is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).''

49	\paragraph{Scope of the \(\bar k\)-base classification.} The chapter restricts
50	to the case where the base field \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed. Over $\bar
51	k\( every closed point is \)\bar k\(-rational, so the ``\)C\( has a \)\bar
52	k$-rational point'' hypothesis of Hartshorne's Exercise~IV.1.3 is automatic
53	(the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) is non-empty,

80	\section{The morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) from two global sections}
81	\begin{lemma}
82	\leanok
83	[Morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) from a globally generating pair of sections]
84	  \label{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections}
85	  
86	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}
87	  Let \(X\) be a scheme over a field \(\bar k\), let \(\mathcal L\) be an invertible
88	  sheaf on \(X\), and let \(s_0, s_1 \in H^0(X, \mathcal L)\) be two global
89	  sections whose common zero locus is empty:
90	  \[
91	    \{x \in X : s_0(x) = 0 \text{ and } s_1(x) = 0\} \;=\; \emptyset.
92	  \]
93	  (Equivalently, \(s_0\) and \(s_1\) generate \(\mathcal L\) at every point.) Then
94	  there exists a unique morphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes
95	  \[
96	    \varphi_{(s_0, s_1)} \colon X \longrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}
97	  \]
98	  whose pullback of \(\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(1)\) is \(\mathcal L\) and whose
99	  pullbacks of the standard coordinate sections $x_0, x_1 \in
100	  H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal O(1))\( are \)s_0\( and \)s_1$ respectively. On the
101	  basic-open chart $D_+(x_0) = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k} \subset
102	  \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\( the morphism \)\varphi_{(s_0, s_1)}\( is the map ``\)x
103	  \mapsto s_1(x) / s_0(x)\(''; on the chart \)D_+(x_1)\( it is the map ``\)x
104	  \mapsto s_0(x) / s_1(x)$''; the two definitions agree on the overlap
105	  \(D_+(x_0) \cap D_+(x_1) = \mathbb G_{m, \bar k}\) by the cocycle identity
106	  \((s_1 / s_0) \cdot (s_0 / s_1) = 1\).
107	\end{lemma}
108	\begin{proof}
109	  This is the standard linear-system construction in the form of
110	  Hartshorne's Theorem~II.7.1 (``Morphisms to \(\mathbb P^n\)''), specialised to
111	  \(n = 1\). Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) ships the corresponding API
112	  under the name
113	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} in
114	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic}:
115	  \begin{quote}
116	  \small
117	  \texttt{def Proj.fromOfGlobalSections \{\(\sigma\) A\} [CommRing A]
118	    [SetLike \(\sigma\) A] [AddSubgroupClass \(\sigma\) A] (\(\mathcal A\) :
119	    \(\mathbb N\) → \(\sigma\)) [GradedRing \(\mathcal A\)] \{X : Scheme\}\\
120	    \phantom{}\quad(f : A →+* X.presheaf.obj \(\top\))\\
121	    \phantom{}\quad(hf : Ideal.map f (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant \(\mathcal A\)).toIdeal = \(\top\)) :\\
122	    \phantom{}\quad(X \(\longrightarrow\) Proj \(\mathcal A\))}
123	  \end{quote}
124	  Specialising to \(\mathcal A = \bar k[x_0, x_1]\) with the standard $\mathbb
125	  N\(-grading by total degree, the inputs are a graded \)\bar k$-algebra
126	  homomorphism \(f \colon \bar k[x_0, x_1] \to H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)\) and a
127	  proof that the irrelevant ideal \((x_0, x_1) \cdot \bar k[x_0, x_1]\) maps to
128	  the unit ideal of \(H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)\) (i.e.\ no common zeros). The
129	  resulting morphism lands in $\mathrm{Proj}\,\bar k[x_0, x_1] = \mathbb
130	  P^1_{\bar k}\( (Hartshorne~II, Example~7.1.3, the construction of \)\mathbb
131	  P^n\( as a \)\mathrm{Proj}\(). When \)\mathcal L$ is non-trivial the present
132	  lemma's signature is reduced to the Mathlib API by working on the open
133	  cover \(X = D(s_0) \cup D(s_1)\), where on each chart the sections become
134	  honest global functions (\(s_1 / s_0\) on \(D(s_0)\), \(s_0 / s_1\) on \(D(s_1)\),
135	  agreeing on the overlap). The chart-glue is performed by the
136	  project-bespoke wrapper
137	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}, whose body
138	  in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean} chains
139	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} with the chart-restriction-and-glue
140	  pattern of \texttt{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}.
141	  Uniqueness follows from the standard separation argument for morphisms to
142	  a separated scheme: two morphisms \(\varphi, \psi \colon X \to \mathbb P^1\)
143	  with \(\varphi^* x_i = \psi^* x_i\) for \(i = 0, 1\) agree on the basic-open
144	  charts \(D_+(x_0), D_+(x_1)\) (where they pull back the affine coordinates to
145	  the same rational function), and these charts cover \(\mathbb P^1\), so the
146	  two morphisms agree globally by gluing.
147	\end{proof}
148	\section{Degree of a morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) via its pole divisor}
149	% NOTE (iter-182 plan-phase Pin 2 signature strengthening). The iter-177
150	% file-skeleton placeholder existential
151	%   "$\exists (d : \mathbb N) (D : C.\mathrm{WeilDivisor}),\
152	%      0 < d \wedge D.\mathrm{degree} = d$"
153	% was vacuous in \(\varphi\) (no constraint tying \(D\) to \(\varphi\) or the
154	% point \(\infty\)). The iter-182 plan-phase refactor (refactor slug
